business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Percentage Actual
description: The actual food cost as a percentage of food sales revenue for the period. Key performance metric for restaurant profitability and cost control. Calculated as (Actual Food Cost / Food Sales Revenue) * 100.

business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Percentage Theoretical
description: The theoretical food cost as a percentage of food sales revenue for the period. Represents the ideal COGS% if all operational standards were met. Calculated as (Theoretical Food Cost / Food Sales Revenue) * 100.
The method used to determine closing inventory value: physical (manual count), perpetual (system-tracked), or hybrid (system with periodic physical verification).
The date and time when this food cost period record was first created in the system. Marks the initiation of the period tracking.

The total food sales revenue (excluding beverage) for the period. Used as the denominator in COGS% calculations. Sourced from Point of Sale (POS) system.
The date and time when this food cost period record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.

The total dollar value of food inventory on hand at the beginning of the period. Represents the starting stock value for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation.

business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Impact Amount
description: The calculated impact of this discount on Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for financial and profitability analysis.

Specifies the daypart(s) during which this discount is valid (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night). Null if no restriction.
Human-readable name or description of the discount as displayed on the receipt and Kitchen Display System (KDS).

The final price after the discount was applied to the item or order.

Indicates whether this discount was voided or removed from the order after initial application.
Number of loyalty program points redeemed to obtain this discount. Null if discount is not loyalty-based.
The maximum allowable discount amount for this promotion or discount type, as configured in the promotion rules.
The minimum order subtotal required to qualify for this discount.
The original price of the item or order subtotal before the discount was applied.
Percentage discount applied to the item or order subtotal. Null if discount is a fixed amount.
Free-text explanation or reason code for the discount, particularly for manager comps and exception-based adjustments.
The calculated impact of this discount on gross revenue for Profit and Loss (P&L) reporting and Same-Store Sales (SSS) analysis.
Indicates whether the discount is applied before tax calculation, after tax calculation, or results in a tax-exempt transaction.
The date and time when this discount record was last modified in the system.
The start date from which this discount or promotion is valid and can be applied.
The end date after which this discount or promotion is no longer valid.
Explanation or reason code for why the discount was voided. Null if discount was not voided.
The date and time when the discount was voided or removed from the order. Null if discount was not voided.
Unique surrogate identifier for each payment tender record associated with a guest order. Serves as the primary key for the order_payment data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

Reference to the FOH (Front of House) employee who processed this payment transaction at the POS terminal. Used for cash accountability, tip reporting, and labor performance analysis.

description: Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: payment.channel is a denormalized STRING storing the service channel at time of payment. Normalizing to channel_id FK referencing order.channel.channel_id eliminates the free-text field and enforces r

Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Daily POS payment settlement and period-close reconciliation require payments to be posted to a specific fiscal period. Restaurant finance teams reconcile tender totals to financial periods for daily
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Needed to post each payment to the appropriate revenue GL account for accurate financial statements.
Reference to the parent guest order for which this payment tender was captured. Supports split-tender scenarios where multiple payment records link to a single order.

Reference to the guest loyalty account from which points or rewards were redeemed as a payment tender. Applicable when tender_type is loyalty_redemption. Links to the loyalty domain for redemption tracking.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Loyalty Program; NRA Loyalty Standards

description: The identifier of the specific POS terminal or payment device on which this transaction was processed. Used for terminal-level reconciliation, hardware fault analysis, and PCI DSS scope management.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Terminal Configuration; PCI DSS v4.0 Req 9.9

description: Foreign key linking to guest.profile. Business justification: Required for payment reconciliation reports linking each payment to the guest profile; essential for fraud detection and loyalty crediting.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.redemption. Business justification: Loyalty tender payment reconciliation: when points or rewards are used as a payment tender, the payment record must reference the redemption for tender-type reporting, loyalty liability accounting, an

The identifier of the original payment transaction being refunded or reversed. Populated only when payment_status is refunded or voided. Enables linkage between the original charge and its corresponding credit for reconciliation.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; GAAP/IFRS Revenue Reversal Standards

description: Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: End-of-shift cash reconciliation and drawer closeout reports require all payments attributed to a specific cashier shift. This is a fundamental daily restaurant operation — shift-level payment totals
Reference to the restaurant location where the payment was captured. Used for site-level financial reconciliation and AUV (Average Unit Volume) reporting.
Reference to the restaurant location where the payment was captured. Used for site-level financial reconciliation and AUV (Average Unit Volume) reporting.

The net amount actually applied from this tender toward the order balance. In split-tender scenarios, this may be less than the tendered_amount. Represents the actual financial obligation satisfied by this tender line.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition; Oracle MICROS POS — Payment Application

description: The alphanumeric approval code returned by the payment processor or card network upon successful authorization of a card-based tender. Used for chargeback resolution, settlement reconciliation, and audit purposes.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Req 3; Visa/Mastercard Authorization Standards

description: The real-world date and time when the payment tender was captured at the Point of Sale (POS) or digital ordering channel. Principal business event timestamp for the payment record. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Transaction Timestamp; PCI DSS v4.0 Req 10.3

description: The method by which card data was captured at the point of interaction. Impacts interchange rates, fraud liability, and PCI DSS compliance scope. Contactless includes NFC/tap-to-pay and mobile wallet transactions.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Req 8; EMV Co. Standards

description: The card network or brand associated with the payment card used for this tender (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, Amex). Used for interchange fee analysis and payment mix reporting.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; Card Network Brand Standards

description: The amount of change to be returned to the guest when the tendered_amount exceeds the applied_amount. Applicable primarily to cash tender transactions. Zero for card and digital payment types.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Cash Drawer Management

description: Audit timestamp recording when this payment record was first written to the data platform. Distinct from captured_timestamp which reflects the real-world payment event time.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements; PCI DSS v4.0 Req 10

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment transaction (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across international restaurant markets.

The meal period or daypart during which the payment was captured. A key dimension in foodservice analytics for revenue distribution, staffing optimization, and promotional performance measurement across breakfast, lunch, dinner, and late-night periods.
reference: NRA — Daypart Classification Standards; Oracle MICROS POS — Daypart Configuration

description: The total monetary discount applied to this payment tender, including promotional discounts, coupon redemptions, and employee meal discounts. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment component for net revenue calculation.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition ASC 606; Oracle MICROS POS — Discount Tracking

description: Masked identifier for the restaurant-issued gift card used in this tender transaction. Retains only partial digits for identification while protecting the full card value. Applicable when tender_type is gift_card.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Req 3.3; NRA Gift Card Best Practices

description: The fee charged by the card-issuing bank for processing this card payment, expressed in the transaction currency. A key component of payment processing cost analysis and COGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) calculations.
reference: Visa/Mastercard Interchange Fee Schedules; GAAP/IFRS Cost Recognition

description: Flag indicating whether this payment record is part of a split-tender transaction where multiple payment instruments were used to satisfy a single order. True when the order has more than one associated payment tender record.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Split Tender Processing

description: Flag indicating whether this payment tender record has been voided prior to settlement. A voided transaction was authorized but cancelled before batch close, distinguishing it from a post-settlement refund.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Void Processing; PCI DSS v4.0

description: The number of loyalty program points redeemed as part of this payment tender. Applicable when tender_type is loyalty_redemption. Used for loyalty liability accounting and program performance analytics.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Loyalty Redemption; GAAP/IFRS Deferred Revenue

description: PCI DSS-compliant masked representation of the payment card number, retaining only the first 4 and last 4 digits (e.g., 4111********1111). Full PANs (Primary Account Numbers) are never stored in the lakehouse per PCI DSS Requirement 3.3.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Req 3.3 — Protect Stored Account Data

description: Indicates whether this payment was authorized in offline mode due to network connectivity loss at the restaurant. Offline authorizations carry elevated fraud and chargeback risk and require special reconciliation handling.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Req 12.3; Oracle MICROS POS — Offline Authorization

description: Current lifecycle state of the payment tender record. Tracks the payment workflow from authorization through settlement or failure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: authorized|captured|settled|declined|voided|refunded|pending — promote to reference product if additional states are needed]
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; Oracle MICROS POS — Payment Lifecycle

description: The externally-known transaction number assigned by the Oracle MICROS POS system at the time of payment capture. Used for end-of-day reconciliation, chargeback research, and audit trails.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Transaction Reference; PCI DSS v4.0 Req 10

description: The name of the payment processing service provider that handled authorization and settlement for this tender (e.g., Worldpay, Heartland, Stripe, Square). Used for processor-level performance and fee analysis.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 — Third-Party Service Provider Management

description: The unique transaction identifier assigned by the external payment processor (e.g., Stripe, Worldpay, Heartland) for this tender. Used for cross-system reconciliation between POS and payment gateway records.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Req 10; Payment Processor Integration Standards

description: The promotional or discount code entered by the guest at the time of payment, if applicable. Used to link payment records to marketing campaign performance and LTO (Limited Time Offer) redemption analytics.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Marketing Campaign Tracking; Oracle MICROS POS — Discount Configuration

description: Categorical reason code explaining why a refund or void was issued for this payment. Used for operational quality analysis, CSAT (Customer Satisfaction) root cause investigation, and fraud monitoring.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Void/Refund Reason Codes; NRA Operational Standards

description: The ISO 8583 or processor-specific response code returned by the payment network for this authorization attempt (e.g., 00 = Approved, 05 = Do Not Honor, 51 = Insufficient Funds). Used for decline analysis and payment success rate monitoring.
reference: ISO 8583 — Financial Transaction Message Standard; PCI DSS v4.0

description: The batch identifier assigned by the payment processor during end-of-day settlement processing. Groups all captured transactions submitted together for bank funding. Used for daily cash reconciliation and SAP S/4HANA AR posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — AR Settlement; PCI DSS v4.0 Req 10

description: The date on which the payment processor settled funds to the restaurant's bank account. Distinct from captured_timestamp. Used for cash flow forecasting, bank reconciliation, and financial close processes in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — GL/AR Settlement; GAAP/IFRS Cash Basis Reporting

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this payment record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-channel reconciliation across Oracle MICROS POS, Olo Digital Ordering Platform, self-service kiosks, and 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) API feeds.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance — Data Lineage Standards

description: Sequential line number identifying the order of tender application within a split-tender transaction. For example, a guest paying part cash and part credit card would have sequence 1 (cash) and sequence 2 (credit card). Enables correct reconstruction of multi-tender payment scenarios.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Split Tender Configuration

description: The total sales tax or VAT amount included in this payment tender, as calculated by the POS system based on applicable jurisdiction tax rules. Required for tax remittance reporting and SAP S/4HANA tax posting.
reference: FDA/USDA Tax Compliance; SAP S/4HANA — Tax Module; GAAP/IFRS

description: Classification of the payment instrument used for this tender. Supports split-tender scenarios. Key dimension for payment mix analysis and financial settlement routing. 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) settlement represents aggregated remittance from delivery partners. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash|credit_card|debit_card|gift_card|loyalty_redemption|3pd_settlement|mobile_wallet|voucher — promote to reference product if additional tender types are needed]
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Tender Type Configuration; PCI DSS v4.0

description: The gross amount presented by the guest for this specific tender line. In cash scenarios, this may exceed the order total, resulting in change due. In card scenarios, this equals the charged amount. Core component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for payment-level financial analysis.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Identifies the 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) aggregator platform responsible for payment settlement when tender_type is 3pd_settlement. Used for partner-level remittance reconciliation and commission tracking.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — 3PD Integration; FTC Franchise Disclosure

description: The order identifier assigned by the 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) partner platform (e.g., DoorDash order ID, Uber Eats order ID). Used to cross-reference restaurant POS records with delivery partner settlement reports.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — 3PD Order Mapping

description: Gratuity amount added by the guest to this payment tender. Tracked separately from the order total for labor distribution, tax reporting, and server tip-out calculations. Applicable in casual and fine-dining service models.
reference: IRS Publication 531 — Reporting Tip Income; GAAP/IFRS

description: A network-issued or processor-issued token that replaces the actual card PAN (Primary Account Number) for secure storage and recurring payment processing. Enables PCI DSS scope reduction through tokenization.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Req 3.5 — Tokenization; EMV Payment Tokenization Specification

description: Audit timestamp recording when this payment record was last modified in the data platform, such as when a status transitions from authorized to settled or a refund is processed.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements; PCI DSS v4.0 Req 10

description: Unique identifier for the refund transaction. Primary key for the order_refund product.

Identifier of the Point of Sale (POS) terminal or workstation where the refund was processed.

Unique surrogate identifier for each tax line record applied to a guest order. Primary key for the order_tax data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Sales tax remittance to tax authorities is period-based. Restaurant tax compliance requires aggregating tax collected by fiscal period for state/local tax filings. Period-level tax reporting is a regu
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Ensures tax liabilities from each order are posted to the correct tax GL account for compliance and reporting.
Reference to the parent guest order to which this tax line belongs. Establishes the header-to-line relationship between guest_order and order_tax.

Foreign key linking to order.order_item. Business justification: Tax records in multi-jurisdiction environments are applied at the line-item level (e.g., food vs. beverage tax rates differ by item). Adding order_item_id to tax enables item-level tax attribution, su
Identifier of the restaurant location where the order was placed. Used to resolve the applicable tax jurisdiction and for location-level financial reconciliation.
Identifier of the restaurant location where the order was placed. Used to resolve the applicable tax jurisdiction and for location-level financial reconciliation.
The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when a post-transaction tax adjustment was applied to this line. Populated only when tax_status = 'adjusted'. Supports audit trail for tax corrections.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Tax Adjustment; GAAP ASC 740

description: The reason code for a post-transaction tax adjustment when tax_status = 'adjusted' (e.g., rate_correction, jurisdiction_change, exemption_applied, refund, audit_correction, other). Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX; IRS Audit Requirements; GAAP ASC 740

description: The computed tax amount for this tax line in the transaction currency (taxable_amount × tax_rate, or as overridden by the tax engine). Core field for financial reconciliation, P&L reporting, and tax remittance.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; IRS Publication 334; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX

description: The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line was computed and applied to the order by the tax engine or POS system. Represents the principal business event time for this tax line record.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Transaction Log; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX

description: Standardized code identifying the tax authority or jurisdiction as defined by the tax engine or ERP (SAP S/4HANA Tax Condition). Used for automated tax remittance and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Tax Configuration; Vertex Tax Engine

description: The governmental jurisdiction level that imposes this tax line. Supports multi-jurisdiction tax compliance across all restaurant locations. Values: federal, state, county, city, district, special.
reference: IRS Publication 15; State Revenue Codes; Local Tax Ordinances

description: The full name of the tax authority or jurisdiction imposing this tax (e.g., 'California State Board of Equalization', 'Cook County Revenue Department', 'City of Chicago'). Used for regulatory reporting and remittance.
reference: State and Local Tax Authority Registries; IRS

description: The SAP S/4HANA cost center code associated with the restaurant location for this tax line. Used for internal cost allocation, P&L reporting by location, and management accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Cost Center Accounting; GAAP/IFRS Management Reporting

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the tax jurisdiction (e.g., 'USA', 'CAN', 'GBR'). Required for international operations, VAT compliance, and cross-border tax reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IFRS IAS 12; VAT Directive 2006/112/EC

description: The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standards; GAAP Audit Trail Requirements

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this tax line (e.g., 'USD', 'CAD', 'GBP'). Required for multi-currency operations and financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS.
The meal period or daypart during which the order was placed (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night, snack). Used for daypart-level tax and revenue analysis in foodservice operations.
reference: NRA Daypart Reporting Standards; Oracle MICROS POS Daypart Configuration

description: Identifies the system or engine that calculated and applied this tax line (e.g., pos = Oracle MICROS POS built-in tax, vertex = Vertex O Series, avalara = Avalara AvaTax, sap_tax = SAP S/4HANA tax engine, manual = manually entered). Used for reconciliation and audit.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Tax Configuration; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX; Vertex O Series

description: Reference number or identifier of the tax exemption certificate presented by the guest or organization. Required for audit documentation when is_exempt is True. Null when no exemption applies.
reference: IRS Publication 557; State Revenue Authority Exemption Certificate Requirements

description: The reason code for a tax exemption when is_exempt is True (e.g., government, non_profit, resale, diplomatic, employee_meal, other). Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance. Null when is_exempt is False.
reference: IRS Tax Exemption Guidelines; State Revenue Codes; FTC Franchise Disclosure Rules

description: Indicates whether this tax line was flagged as exempt (True) due to a guest exemption certificate, government order, or qualifying non-profit status. When True, tax_amount should be zero and exemption_reason should be populated.
reference: IRS Tax Exemption Guidelines; State Revenue Codes; USDA Exemption Rules

description: Indicates whether the tax amount on this line has been refunded to the guest (True) as part of an order refund or cancellation. Used for refund reconciliation and tax reversal tracking.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Refund Processing; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Reversal; GAAP ASC 606

description: Indicates whether this tax was already included in the menu price (True) or added at checkout on top of the menu price (False). Critical for VAT/GST jurisdictions and for accurate revenue recognition and tax remittance calculations.

Sequential number ordering multiple tax lines within the same order (e.g., 1 = state sales tax, 2 = county tax, 3 = city beverage tax). Ensures deterministic ordering for receipt display and financial reporting.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Tax Line Sequencing; NRA Receipt Standards

description: The service channel through which the order was placed (pos = in-store Point of Sale, drive_thru = Drive-Thru (DT), olo = Online Ordering (OLO), third_party_delivery = Third-Party Delivery (3PD), kiosk = self-service kiosk, catering). Used for channel-level tax analysis and compliance.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; NRA Channel Reporting Standards

description: The tax amount as originally computed at the time of order placement, before any post-transaction adjustments. Populated when tax_status = 'adjusted' to preserve the audit trail of the original calculation.
reference: GAAP ASC 740; IFRS IAS 12; IRS Audit Requirements

description: The business date (yyyy-MM-dd) to which this tax line is attributed for tax remittance and financial period reporting. May differ from the transaction timestamp for end-of-day batch processing or period adjustments.
reference: GAAP ASC 740; IFRS IAS 12; IRS Tax Period Rules

business_glossary_term: Point of Sale (POS) Tax Line Reference
description: The native tax line identifier or sequence number assigned by Oracle MICROS POS for this tax entry. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the POS transaction log and the lakehouse Silver Layer.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Tax Transaction Log

description: The applicable tax rate expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.0875 for 8.75%). Sourced from the tax engine or POS tax configuration at the time of the transaction. Used for tax amount validation and audit.
reference: State and Local Tax Authority Rate Tables; SAP S/4HANA Tax Condition Records

description: The tax amount refunded to the guest when is_refunded is True. May be a partial refund (less than tax_amount) for partial order refunds. Used for financial reconciliation and tax reversal reporting.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Refund Processing; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX; GAAP ASC 606

description: The fiscal period (e.g., '2024-Q1', '2024-03') in which this tax amount is scheduled for remittance to the tax authority. Used for tax filing calendar management and period-end close.
reference: IRS Tax Filing Calendar; State Revenue Authority Filing Schedules; GAAP ASC 740

description: Indicates whether this tax amount has been remitted to the applicable tax authority (pending = not yet remitted, remitted = successfully paid to authority, not_applicable = exempt or zero-rate, failed = remittance attempt failed). Supports tax compliance tracking.
reference: IRS Tax Remittance Requirements; State Revenue Authority Filing Rules; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX

description: The SAP S/4HANA FI-TX tax document number associated with this tax line for GL posting and tax remittance. Enables end-to-end traceability from POS transaction to ERP financial posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Tax Document; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting Standards

description: The operational system of record from which this tax line record originated (micros_pos = Oracle MICROS POS, sap_s4hana = SAP S/4HANA FI-TX, olo = Olo Digital Ordering Platform, manual = manually entered). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; SAP S/4HANA; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Data Lineage Standards

description: Two-letter US state code (or equivalent provincial code) of the tax jurisdiction where the restaurant is located (e.g., 'IL', 'CA', 'TX'). Used for state-level tax rate lookup, remittance routing, and regulatory reporting.
reference: ANSI INCITS 38; IRS State Tax Authority Codes; State Revenue Departments

description: The internal or POS-assigned tax code identifying the specific tax rule applied (e.g., 'TX_CA_SALES', 'TX_CHI_BEV'). Maps to the tax condition record in SAP S/4HANA and Oracle MICROS POS tax configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Tax Condition Records; Oracle MICROS POS Tax Configuration

description: Human-readable name of the tax as displayed on the guest receipt or financial report (e.g., 'Illinois Sales Tax', 'Chicago Beverage Tax', 'CA Bag Fee'). Required for receipt compliance and guest transparency.
reference: NRA Receipt Compliance Guidelines; State Consumer Protection Laws

description: Current lifecycle status of this tax line record. 'applied' = tax successfully computed and applied; 'voided' = tax line cancelled due to order void; 'adjusted' = tax amount corrected post-transaction; 'pending' = awaiting tax engine resolution; 'reversed' = tax reversed for refund.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Tax Document Status; Oracle MICROS POS Void Processing

description: The category of tax applied to this line (e.g., sales_tax, vat, beverage_tax, bag_fee, excise_tax, service_tax). Supports differentiated tax reporting and compliance across jurisdictions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_tax|vat|beverage_tax|bag_fee|excise_tax|service_tax|surcharge|environmental_fee — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
reference: FDA Food Labeling Regulations; State Revenue Codes; IRS Publication 334

description: The portion of the order subtotal that is subject to this specific tax line, expressed in the transaction currency. Excludes non-taxable items, exempt amounts, and tax-inclusive components. Used for tax calculation validation.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; IRS Publication 334; State Revenue Codes

description: The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental loads, and audit trail.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standards; Databricks Delta Lake CDC

description: The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line was voided, if applicable. Populated only when tax_status = 'voided'. Used for void audit trail and financial reversal processing.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Void Processing; SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Reversal

Type of enrollment action. New indicates first-time enrollment; re_enrollment indicates returning member; transfer indicates program migration; upgrade indicates tier promotion enrollment.

Source that drove the enrollment. Organic for unprompted; referral_code for member referral; campaign for marketing campaign; social_media for social channels; email for email marketing; in_store for restaurant promotion; partner for third-party partner. Indicates whether a welcome offer or sign-up bonus was issued to the member upon enrollment. True if issued; False if not issued.

Indicates whether the member has consented to sharing their data with third-party partners for marketing purposes. True if opted in, False if opted out.

business_glossary_term: Combo Meal Component - Combo Meal Component Id
description: Primary key for the combo_meal_component association

business_glossary_term: Combo Meal Component - Combo Meal Id
description: Foreign key linking this component record to its parent combo meal definition.

business_glossary_term: Combo Meal Component - Menu Item Id
description: Foreign key linking this component record to the specific menu item that serves as a component in the combo.

The caloric value (kcal) attributed to this specific component within the combo meal, used to compute the combo's total and range calorie display. Belongs to the pairing because the same item may contribute different calories in different combo contexts (e.g., different portion sizes).

Ordinal position of this menu item within the combo meal's component list. Drives display order on menu boards, POS screens, and digital ordering interfaces. Belongs to the pairing, not to either entity alone.
Functional role of this menu item within the combo meal bundle (e.g., ENTREE, SIDE, BEVERAGE). The same menu item may play different roles in different combos. Belongs to the pairing.

The standard cost of goods (COGS) allocated to this component within the combo meal bundle, used for food cost and margin analysis at the combo level. Belongs to the pairing, not to the item or combo alone.

Indicates whether this menu item can be included as a component in a combo meal offering. Used by menu engineering teams to configure combo meal bundles and upsell prompts in POS and OLO. [Moved from menu_item: This boolean flag on menu_item indicates whether an item CAN participate in a combo, but the actual participation — which combo, in what role, in what quantity — is now captured in combo_meal_component. The eligibility flag is a derived or redundant signal once the association table exists. It may be retained on menu_item as a denormalized convenience flag for filtering, but architecturally the ground truth of combo participation lives in the association. Teams should evaluate whether to deprecate it or keep it as a computed/maintained flag.]
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Combo Configuration; NRA Menu Engineering

description: Indicates whether this component is mandatory in the combo meal. If FALSE, the guest may omit this item without invalidating the combo. Belongs to the pairing.
Indicates whether the guest may substitute this component with an eligible alternative menu item. Drives substitution logic in POS and OLO systems. Belongs to the pairing.

Number of units of this menu item included in the combo meal at the standard bundle price. For example, a Family Pack may include 4 units of a burger. Belongs to the pairing, not to the item or combo alone.

business_glossary_term: Menu Item Assignment - Menu Item Assignment Id
description: Primary key for the menu_item_assignment association
Foreign key linking to the published menu record that this assignment belongs to

business_glossary_term: Menu Item Assignment - Menu Item Id
description: Foreign key linking to the menu item being assigned to the menu

Channel-specific availability override for this item on this menu, expressed as a pipe-delimited list of enabled channels (e.g., 'DT|OLO'). Overrides the item-level channel flags when a menu engineer restricts or expands availability for a specific menu context. Belongs to the assignment because channel restrictions may differ per menu.

The category label under which this item is grouped on this specific menu (e.g., 'Burgers', 'Sides', 'Beverages'). Belongs to the assignment because the same item may be categorized differently across menus (e.g., a chicken sandwich may appear under 'Sandwiches' on one menu and 'Value Meals' on another).

The calendar date on which this item is retired and no longer available on this specific menu. Enables item sunsets and LTO expirations within a menu version. Belongs to the assignment because the same item may be retired from one menu while remaining active on others.

The calendar date on which this item becomes active and available for ordering on this specific menu. Enables phased item introductions and LTO launches within a menu version without requiring a full menu republish. Belongs to the assignment because the same item may have different activation dates on different menus.

Indicates whether this item is promoted or highlighted on this specific menu (e.g., shown in a featured section, displayed with a badge, or given visual prominence on digital menu boards). Belongs to the assignment because featured status is a menu-context decision, not an intrinsic item property.

Menu-specific selling price for this item that overrides the item's base_price on menu_item. Used for channel-specific pricing (e.g., 3PD surcharge menus), franchise-specific pricing, or LTO promotional pricing. Belongs to the assignment because the same item may be priced differently across menus. Null indicates no override — base_price applies.

The display sequence position of this menu item within its display category on this menu. Controls the order in which items are rendered on digital menu boards, OLO interfaces, and POS screens. Belongs to the assignment because the same item may appear in different positions on different menus.

description: Indicates whether this menu item is available for dine-in service. Used to configure FOH (Front of House) menu boards and server-facing POS screens.
Indicates whether this menu item is available for sale through the Drive-Thru (DT) channel. Used to configure channel-specific menus in Oracle MICROS POS and drive-thru menu boards.

Indicates whether this menu item is certified or designated as gluten-free per FDA gluten-free labeling standards (less than 20 ppm gluten). Used for allergen filtering on digital ordering platforms and guest safety disclosures.
reference: FDA Gluten-Free Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101.91); FALCPA

description: Indicates whether this menu item is a Limited Time Offer (LTO). LTO items have defined availability windows and are used for promotional campaigns, seasonal offerings, and new product testing. Drives marketing campaign execution and supply chain planning.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — LTO Configuration; Salesforce CRM — Campaign Management

description: Indicates whether this menu item is available through the Online Ordering (OLO) digital channel. Controls item visibility on the Olo Digital Ordering Platform for web and mobile ordering.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Menu Availability

description: Indicates whether this menu item is subject to sales tax. Tax applicability varies by jurisdiction and item type (e.g., prepared food vs. grocery). Drives tax calculation in Oracle MICROS POS and SAP S/4HANA AR.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Tax Configuration; SAP S/4HANA SD Tax; GAAP/IFRS

description: Indicates whether this menu item meets vegan dietary criteria (no animal products including dairy and eggs). Used for dietary filter functionality on OLO, 3PD platforms, and in-restaurant menu boards.
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Rule; NRA Dietary Disclosure Guidelines

description: Indicates whether this menu item meets vegetarian dietary criteria (no meat, poultry, or seafood). Used for dietary filter functionality on OLO, 3PD platforms, and in-restaurant menu boards.
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Rule; NRA Dietary Disclosure Guidelines

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the menu item used across POS, KDS, ERP, and supply chain systems. Equivalent to the PLU (Price Look-Up) code in Oracle MICROS POS.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — PLU Code; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Number

description: Long-form marketing and operational description of the menu item including key ingredients, preparation style, and flavor profile. Used on digital menus, OLO platform, 3PD integrations, and guest-facing materials.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Item Description; FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101)

description: The customer-facing display name of the menu item as it appears on the menu board, POS screen, and digital ordering channels (DT, OLO, 3PD).
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Item Master; FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101)

description: Current lifecycle status of the menu item. 'active' = currently sold; 'inactive' = temporarily removed; 'lto' = Limited Time Offer (LTO) with defined availability window; 'discontinued' = permanently removed; 'pending_launch' = approved but not yet live; 'suspended' = pulled due to safety or supply issue.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Item Status; FDA Menu Labeling Rule

description: The date this menu item was first made available for sale across the restaurant network. Used for item lifecycle tracking, PMIX trend analysis, and new product performance reporting.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Item Effective Date; NRA Menu Engineering

description: The date on which a Limited Time Offer (LTO) menu item is removed from sale. Null for non-LTO items. Triggers automatic item deactivation in POS and removal from OLO and 3PD channel menus.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — LTO Scheduling; Salesforce CRM — Campaign Calendar

description: The date on which a Limited Time Offer (LTO) menu item becomes available for sale. Null for non-LTO items. Used to coordinate marketing campaign launches, supply chain procurement, and POS menu activation.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — LTO Scheduling; Salesforce CRM — Campaign Calendar

description: Menu engineering quadrant classification based on the item's profitability and popularity: 'star' (high profit, high popularity), 'plow_horse' (low profit, high popularity), 'puzzle' (high profit, low popularity), 'dog' (low profit, low popularity). Used by menu development teams to make pricing and promotion decisions.
reference: NRA Menu Engineering Standards; Kasavana & Smith Menu Engineering Model

description: Standard portion weight of the menu item in grams as defined in the recipe and BOM (Bill of Materials). Used for food cost control, yield management, waste tracking, and FDA nutrition label compliance.
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101); USDA Food Composition Standards; MarketMan BOM

description: Standard preparation time for this menu item measured in seconds, from order receipt on the KDS (Kitchen Display System) to item ready. Used for Speed of Service (SOS) benchmarking, Ticket Time targets, and kitchen throughput planning.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — KDS Prep Time; NRA Speed of Service Standards

description: Identifies which restaurant format(s) this menu item is eligible for: QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant), casual dining, fine dining, or all formats. Governs menu item availability across the restaurant portfolio.
reference: FranConnect — Restaurant Format Classification; Oracle MICROS POS

description: Required serving temperature classification for this menu item: hot, cold, ambient, or frozen. Critical for HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) compliance, food safety SOPs, and Zenput audit checklists.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); HACCP Guidelines; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) code that links the sellable menu item to its corresponding inventory and procurement record in MarketMan and SAP S/4HANA MM. Enables COGS tracking and PAR level management.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management — SKU Master; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Sodium content of the menu item in milligrams (mg) per standard serving. Required for FDA nutrition disclosure and supports guest health-conscious ordering decisions on digital platforms.
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101.11); USDA Dietary Guidelines

description: Numeric sort order controlling the display sequence of this item within its category on POS screens, digital menu boards, OLO, and 3PD platforms. Lower values appear first. Used by menu engineering teams to influence guest ordering behavior.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Item Sort Order; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: The native item identifier from the operational source system (Oracle MICROS POS) from which this record was ingested into the lakehouse Silver layer. Enables lineage tracing and reconciliation between the lakehouse and the POS system of record.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Item Master; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage

description: Secondary classification within the item category for granular PMIX analysis and menu engineering (e.g., Burger, Chicken Sandwich, Fountain Drink, Milkshake, French Fries). Supports drill-down reporting in P&L and product mix analytics.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Subcategory; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

description: Tax category code assigned to this menu item in SAP S/4HANA for jurisdiction-specific tax rate determination. Maps to the tax condition type in SD pricing. Examples: FOOD_PREP, BEVERAGE_ALCO, BEVERAGE_NON_ALCO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Tax Condition Types; GAAP/IFRS; Local Tax Regulations

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this menu item record in the system of record. Used to detect changes for incremental lakehouse ingestion and to track menu item modification history. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Record Audit; ISO 8601

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each Limited Time Offer (LTO) record. Primary key for the lto data product.

Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: LTO launch approval is a formal menu governance process requiring sign-off from a named menu director or VP. Tracing approvals to a specific employee supports governance audits and accountability in m
Reference to the associated marketing campaign in Salesforce CRM that supports this LTO. Links LTO performance data to marketing spend, campaign reach, and promotional ROI analytics.

Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: LTO P&L reporting requires anchoring each LTO to a fiscal period for revenue, food cost, and marketing spend variance analysis. Foodservice finance teams produce LTO post-launch financial reviews by f

Foreign key linking to foodsafety.food_safety_audit. Business justification: LTO launch approval workflows require a food safety audit sign-off before a new item goes live. menu_lto has a 'food_safety_approved' flag that is a denormalized signal; a direct FK to the approving f
Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: LTO rollout tracking per franchisee needs to associate each limited‑time offer with the responsible franchisee.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.offer. Business justification: LTO loyalty offer coordination: QSR operators pair LTO launches with specific loyalty offers (e.g., double points during launch week). Marketing operations require this link to execute integrated LTO+

Foreign key linking to menu.menu. Business justification: A Limited Time Offer is published on a specific menu (e.g., the national QSR lunch menu, the OLO digital menu, or a regional franchise menu). menu_lto currently has no FK to the menu product, creating

Reference to the primary menu item record associated with this LTO in the menu domain. For bundle LTOs, references the parent bundle item. Links LTO governance to the underlying menu item master data including BOM, nutritional data, and allergen declarations.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management; Internal Menu Domain

description: Foreign key linking to realestate.nro_project. Business justification: Grand opening LTO planning is a named QSR business process: NRO project teams coordinate with marketing to launch location-specific LTOs tied to new restaurant openings. This link enables grand openin

Self-referencing identifier pointing to the prior LTO record if this is a returning or re-run offer. Enables lineage tracking of recurring LTOs, year-over-year performance comparison, and fan-favorite lifecycle analysis.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering Framework; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Foreign key linking to marketing.promotion. Business justification: LTO promotion linkage: every LTO item is backed by a specific promotion defining its discount mechanics and eligibility. Marketing and menu teams must link menu_lto to its governing promotion for laun

Foreign key linking to supply.supplier_contract. Business justification: LTO launch approval gate: menu planning and procurement teams must validate that a supplier contract covers the full LTO window (planned_launch_date to planned_end_date) before approving launch. This

Foreign key linking to guest.guest_segment. Business justification: LTO launches are planned and evaluated against specific guest segments (e.g., loyalty members, value seekers). Linking menu_lto to guest_segment enables segment-targeted rollout planning and post-laun
Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Needed to track LTO launch and performance per restaurant unit for regional rollout analysis.

The actual calendar date on which the LTO was removed from menus. May differ from planned_end_date due to early discontinuation, extended popularity, or supply constraints. Used for LTO lifecycle duration analysis.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management; Internal Audit Trail

description: The actual calendar date on which the LTO went live in restaurants. May differ from planned_launch_date due to supply chain delays, regulatory approvals, or operational readiness issues. Used for post-launch performance analysis.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management; Internal Audit Trail

description: Indicates whether a formal allergen review has been completed (True) for this LTO item, confirming all major allergens (per FDA FALCPA — Big 9 allergens) are identified and declared. Required for regulatory compliance and guest safety.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); ISO 22000; HACCP

description: Calendar date on which the LTO received final business approval to proceed. Used to measure ideation-to-approval cycle time and track governance compliance.
reference: Internal Menu Development SOP; Internal Governance Framework

description: Current approval workflow status of the LTO concept: pending (awaiting review), approved (cleared for production), rejected (not approved for launch), or on_hold (paused pending additional information). Distinct from lifecycle_status which tracks operational deployment state.
reference: Internal Menu Development SOP; Zenput Operational Compliance

description: Internal narrative describing the culinary concept, inspiration, and strategic rationale behind the LTO. Used by Menu Development and Engineering teams during ideation and approval stages.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code identifying the primary country of LTO deployment (e.g., 'USA', 'CAN', 'GBR'). Supports international menu localization, regulatory compliance tracking, and cross-border PMIX analysis.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; FDA Food Labeling Regulations; USDA Inspection Standards

description: The date and time when this LTO record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail entry point for the LTO lifecycle and supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.

The primary daypart during which the LTO is intended to be sold and promoted. Daypart segmentation drives kitchen prep scheduling, labor planning, and marketing campaign timing. Aligns with Oracle MICROS POS daypart configuration.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Daypart Configuration; NRA Operational Standards

description: Forecasted number of LTO units expected to be sold per restaurant per week during the active offer period. Used for supply chain procurement planning, PAR level setting in MarketMan, and labor scheduling in Planday.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management; Planday Workforce Scheduling; Internal Demand Planning

description: Indicates whether the LTO item has received food safety clearance (True) per HACCP protocols and FDA/USDA regulatory requirements before launch. Tracked via Zenput food safety audit workflows. No LTO may go active without this flag being True.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); USDA FSIS; HACCP; ISO 22000; Zenput Food Safety Audits

description: Indicates whether this LTO is a full national launch (True) as opposed to a regional, franchise group, or test market deployment (False). Used for executive reporting, national marketing budget allocation, and supply chain capacity planning.
reference: Internal Menu Development SOP; FranConnect Franchise Development

description: Indicates whether this LTO features an item that has been previously offered (True) or is a brand-new menu introduction (False). Supports fan-favorite tracking, nostalgia marketing strategies, and historical PMIX benchmarking.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering Framework; Salesforce CRM Campaign Management

description: Indicates whether this LTO is currently in a limited test market phase (True) or has been deployed beyond test market scope (False). Used to segregate test performance data from production PMIX reporting.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering Framework; NRA Industry Standards

description: Number of calendar days between the LTO approval date and the planned launch date. Used by supply chain and procurement teams to validate that sufficient lead time exists for ingredient sourcing, supplier onboarding, and distribution via Coupa Procurement.
reference: Coupa Procurement; SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Internal Supply Chain SOP

description: Current state of the LTO in its end-to-end lifecycle: ideation (concept under development), approved (greenlit for production), in_test (active in test markets only), active (live in production rollout), sunset (offer period ended naturally), cancelled (discontinued before planned end).
reference: Internal Menu Development SOP; FranConnect Compliance Tracking

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the LTO, used in Oracle MICROS POS menu management, Salesforce CRM campaign references, and Olo Digital Ordering Platform configurations. Serves as the cross-system reference key.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management; Internal Naming Convention

description: Classification of the LTO by its nature: new_item (first-time introduction), returning_item (previously offered item brought back), bundle (combination of existing items at a promotional price), promotional_price (existing item at a temporary price), or seasonal (tied to a calendar season or holiday).
reference: Internal Menu Engineering Framework; NRA Industry Standards

description: Short consumer-facing promotional tagline or headline used in advertising, in-restaurant POP (Point of Purchase) materials, and digital marketing for this LTO (e.g., 'Back by Popular Demand — For a Limited Time Only!'). Sourced from Salesforce CRM campaign assets.
reference: Salesforce CRM Marketing Automation; FTC Advertising Regulations

description: Consumer-facing marketing name of the Limited Time Offer as it appears on menus, POS displays, digital ordering platforms, and promotional materials (e.g., 'Spicy Crispy Chicken Sandwich LTO').
reference: NRA Menu Labeling Standards; Oracle MICROS POS

description: Indicates whether the LTO item's nutritional profile has been reviewed and approved (True) for menu labeling compliance per FDA menu labeling regulations (21 CFR Part 101). Required before consumer-facing publication.
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101); NRA ServSafe Standards

description: The item identifier assigned to this LTO in the Olo Digital Ordering Platform for online ordering and third-party delivery (3PD) channel activation. Used to synchronize LTO availability across digital channels.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Digital Channel Management

description: Indicates whether the LTO applies to franchise-operated restaurants, company-owned restaurants, or both. Governs FranConnect compliance tracking, royalty rate applicability, and franchise disclosure obligations under FTC regulations.
reference: FranConnect Franchise Development; FTC Franchise Disclosure Regulations; IFA Best Practices

description: Total number of calendar days the LTO is planned to be active, calculated as the difference between planned_end_date and planned_launch_date. Used for supply chain volume forecasting, marketing campaign budgeting, and franchisee communication planning.
reference: Internal Menu Development SOP; MarketMan Inventory Management

description: The originally scheduled calendar date on which the LTO was planned to be removed from menus. Drives supply chain wind-down, inventory PAR level adjustments, and marketing campaign sunset planning.
reference: Internal Menu Development SOP; MarketMan Inventory Management

description: The originally scheduled calendar date on which the LTO was planned to go live in restaurants. Used for supply chain procurement lead time planning, marketing campaign scheduling, and NRO coordination.
reference: Internal Menu Development SOP; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: The target Product Mix (PMIX) percentage representing the share of total transactions this LTO is expected to capture during its active period. Used by Menu Engineering to set performance benchmarks and evaluate LTO success post-launch.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS PMIX Reporting; Internal Menu Engineering Framework

description: The item code assigned to this LTO in Oracle MICROS POS for order capture, menu display, and PMIX reporting. This is the operational identifier used by cashiers, KDS (Kitchen Display System), and sales reporting.

Geographic region code identifying the market(s) where this LTO is deployed (e.g., 'US-NORTHEAST', 'EMEA', 'APAC'). Used for regional rollout scoping, localized marketing campaign alignment, and regional P&L reporting.
reference: FranConnect Site Selection; Internal Geographic Hierarchy

description: The restaurant service format(s) for which this LTO is applicable: qsr (Quick-Service Restaurant), casual_dining, fine_dining, or all_formats. Governs which restaurant types are eligible to activate the LTO in Oracle MICROS POS.
reference: Internal Restaurant Operations SOP; NRA Industry Classification

description: Geographic and operational scope of the LTO deployment: national (all restaurants), regional (specific geographic region), franchise_group (specific franchisee group), company_owned (company-operated units only), or test_market (limited pilot locations). Governs FranConnect franchise compliance and royalty tracking.
reference: FranConnect Franchise Development; IFA Franchise Best Practices

description: The seasonal period or consumer occasion this LTO is designed to capitalize on (e.g., 'Summer 2025', 'Super Bowl', 'Halloween', 'Valentine's Day', 'Back to School'). Supports marketing calendar planning and year-over-year seasonal PMIX trend analysis.
reference: Internal Marketing Calendar; Salesforce CRM Campaign Management

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this LTO record originated: oracle_micros_pos (menu management), salesforce_crm (campaign-driven LTO creation), franconnect (franchise-initiated LTO), olo (digital channel LTO), or manual (direct data entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; Internal Data Lineage Framework

description: The recommended consumer-facing selling price for the LTO item or bundle, expressed in the local operating currency (USD for domestic operations). Used as the baseline for franchisee pricing guidance and competitive pricing analysis. Actual price may vary by market.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting Standards; FTC Franchise Disclosure Regulations

description: The primary ordering channel(s) for which the LTO is available: dine_in (Front of House), drive_thru (DT), olo (Online Ordering via Olo Digital Ordering Platform), third_party_delivery (3PD aggregators), or all_channels. Governs channel-specific menu activation in Oracle MICROS POS and Olo.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Oracle MICROS POS Channel Management

business_glossary_term: Target Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%)
description: The planned food cost as a percentage of the LTO selling price, representing the target CoGS% for this offer. Used by Menu Engineering to evaluate profitability and ensure the LTO meets financial thresholds before approval.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; Internal Menu Engineering Framework; GAAP/IFRS

description: The date and time when this LTO record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to detect changes, trigger downstream refresh processes, and support audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Unique surrogate identifier for each modifier option record in the master modifier catalog. Serves as the primary key for all downstream references from Oracle MICROS POS and Olo Digital Ordering Platform.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Modifiers (e.g., add‑ons) are defined by a chef or manager; tracking creator aids cost and allergen management.

Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: Modifier COGS and 86 suppression: each modifier option (e.g., add avocado, substitute protein) maps to a specific ingredient for food cost calculation and automatic POS/OLO suppression when that ingre
Reference to the parent menu item to which this modifier is applicable. Enables PMIX (Product Mix) analysis and menu engineering decisions at the item-modifier level.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module

description: Reference to the modifier group to which this modifier option belongs (e.g., 'Cheese Options', 'Bun Type', 'Size'). Establishes the parent-child grouping used by Oracle MICROS POS for menu customization display.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: Modifiers (extra cheese, bacon add-on, sauce swap) consume specific stock items. This FK enables modifier-level inventory depletion, food cost tracking per modifier, and automatic OLO/POS suppression

Pipe-delimited list of major food allergens introduced or removed by this modifier, per FDA's 9 major allergens (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree_nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame). Example: 'milk|wheat'. Critical for guest safety, HACCP compliance, and allergen declaration on digital ordering platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: milk|eggs|fish|shellfish|tree_nuts|peanuts|wheat|soybeans|sesame — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); FDA FSMA; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Name or employee identifier of the menu engineering or culinary team member who approved this modifier for inclusion in the menu catalog. Supports governance, audit trail, and change management for menu updates.
reference: Internal Menu Governance Policy; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: The date on which this modifier was formally approved by the menu engineering team for deployment to production POS and digital ordering systems.
reference: Internal Menu Governance Policy; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Pipe-delimited list of ordering channels where this modifier is available (e.g., 'dine_in|drive_thru|olo|3pd'). Supports channel-specific menu configuration for DT (Drive-Thru), OLO (Online Ordering), and 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dine_in|drive_thru|olo|3pd|kiosk|catering — promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Pipe-delimited list of dayparts during which this modifier is available (e.g., 'breakfast|lunch|dinner|late_night'). Enables daypart-specific menu engineering and ensures modifiers are only offered during operationally appropriate time windows.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; NRA Daypart Standards

description: The incremental calorie change (in kilocalories) added to or subtracted from the base menu item's calorie count when this modifier is selected. Positive for additions, negative for removals. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance and nutritional transparency on digital ordering channels.
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101); FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA)

description: Incremental change in total carbohydrate content (in grams) when this modifier is applied. Required for FDA nutritional labeling and supports guest dietary needs (e.g., low-carb, diabetic-friendly options).
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101); USDA Nutritional Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Modifier Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Delta
description: The incremental raw ingredient cost (COGS) associated with applying this modifier, expressed in the local currency. Used for menu engineering profitability analysis, CoGS% (Cost of Goods Sold Percentage) calculations, and P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting at the modifier level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; GAAP/IFRS Cost Accounting; Internal Menu Engineering Policy

description: The timestamp when this modifier record was first created in the menu management system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the price_delta (e.g., 'USD', 'CAD', 'GBP'). Supports multi-currency operations across international franchise markets.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting Standards

description: The date after which this modifier record is no longer valid in the menu catalog. Null for currently active modifiers. Enables historical menu reconstruction for PMIX analysis, audit, and regulatory reporting.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering Policy; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

description: The date from which this modifier record is valid and active in the menu catalog. Supports temporal menu versioning, NRO (New Restaurant Opening) menu rollouts, and historical PMIX (Product Mix) analysis.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering Policy; Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module

description: Incremental change in total fat content (in grams) when this modifier is applied to the base menu item. Required for FDA nutritional labeling compliance on menus and digital ordering platforms.
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101); USDA Nutritional Guidelines

description: URL pointing to the digital image asset for this modifier, used on kiosk displays, OLO (Online Ordering) platforms, and 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) integrations to visually represent the modifier option to guests.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Internal Digital Asset Management Policy

description: Indicates whether this modifier introduces zero major allergens (True) or introduces at least one allergen (False). Used for rapid allergen filtering on digital ordering platforms and guest-facing allergen transparency tools.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); ISO 22000

description: Real-time availability flag indicating whether this modifier is currently orderable (True) or temporarily unavailable due to ingredient stockout, 86'd item, or operational hold (False). Distinct from status, which reflects the lifecycle state; this flag reflects operational availability at a point in time.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; MarketMan Inventory Management

description: Indicates whether this modifier is pre-selected by default when a guest orders the parent menu item (True) or must be explicitly chosen (False). Controls default state in Oracle MICROS POS and Olo Digital Ordering Platform order flows.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

business_glossary_term: Modifier Is Limited Time Offer (LTO) Flag
description: Indicates whether this modifier is part of a Limited Time Offer (LTO) promotion (True) or is a permanent menu option (False). LTO modifiers are subject to promotional campaign timelines and require coordinated activation/deactivation across all channels.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering Policy; NRA Industry Standards

description: Indicates whether a selection from this modifier's group is mandatory before the order can be completed (True) or optional (False). For example, selecting a size may be required for a beverage item. Enforced at POS and digital ordering checkout.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: The date on which this modifier's LTO availability expires. Null for permanent modifiers. Triggers automatic deactivation in POS and digital ordering systems upon expiry.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering Policy; Marketing Campaign Management

description: The date on which this modifier becomes available as part of a Limited Time Offer (LTO) campaign. Null for permanent modifiers. Used to schedule activation in Oracle MICROS POS and Olo Digital Ordering Platform.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering Policy; Marketing Campaign Management

description: Maximum number of times this modifier can be applied to a single menu item in one order (e.g., a guest can add up to 3 extra cheese slices). Enforced at POS and digital ordering checkout to control portion and cost.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Internal Operations SOP

description: Detailed description of the modifier option for internal documentation, menu engineering analysis, and digital channel display (e.g., online ordering, third-party delivery apps).
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; NRA Industry Standards

description: Human-readable display name of the modifier option as it appears on POS terminals, KDS (Kitchen Display System), and digital ordering channels (e.g., 'Extra Cheese', 'No Onions', 'Gluten-Free Bun', 'Large Size').
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Current lifecycle status of the modifier option. 'active' indicates available for ordering; 'inactive' is temporarily unavailable; 'discontinued' is permanently removed; 'pending_approval' is awaiting menu engineering sign-off; 'seasonal' is available only during specific periods.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Internal Menu Governance Policy

description: Categorical classification of the modifier's operational intent. 'addition' adds an ingredient (e.g., Extra Cheese), 'removal' removes an ingredient (e.g., No Onions), 'substitution' replaces an ingredient (e.g., Gluten-Free Bun), 'size_upsize'/'size_downsize' changes portion size, 'preparation' specifies cooking instruction (e.g., Well Done). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: addition|removal|substitution|size_upsize|size_downsize|preparation — promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; NRA Industry Standards

description: Price Look-Up code assigned to this modifier in Oracle MICROS POS for transaction processing and sales reporting. Used for PMIX (Product Mix) reporting and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) tracking at the modifier level.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; NRA Industry Standards

description: Hexadecimal color code for the modifier's button on Oracle MICROS POS touchscreen terminals. Used to visually differentiate modifier categories (e.g., additions in green, removals in red) to improve FOH (Front of House) order accuracy and speed of service (SOS).
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; Internal FOH SOP

description: Specific BOH (Back of House) preparation instruction associated with this modifier, transmitted to the KDS (Kitchen Display System) when the modifier is selected (e.g., 'Grill well done', 'Toast bun', 'Serve sauce on side'). Supports SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) compliance in kitchen operations.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS KDS Integration; Internal BOH SOP

description: The incremental price adjustment applied to the base menu item price when this modifier is selected. Positive values represent upcharges (e.g., +1.50 for Extra Cheese); negative values represent discounts or removals (e.g., -0.50 for No Protein). Zero indicates no price change. Used by Oracle MICROS POS for real-time transaction pricing.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module; GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition

description: Incremental change in protein content (in grams) when this modifier is applied. Supports FDA nutritional labeling requirements and enables protein-focused menu analytics for health-oriented menu engineering.
reference: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101); USDA Nutritional Guidelines

description: Identifies the restaurant format(s) for which this modifier is applicable: QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant), casual dining, fine dining, or all formats. Supports format-specific menu configuration across the franchise and company-owned portfolio.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering Policy; FranConnect Franchise Management

description: Abbreviated display name used on KDS (Kitchen Display System) screens and printed receipts where space is constrained (e.g., 'XTR CHSE', 'NO ONI', 'GF BUN').
reference: Oracle MICROS POS Menu Management Module

Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: modifier_group.is_franchise_configurable indicates franchisee-specific modifier configurations exist as a named business process (franchise menu customization governance). A franchisee_id FK identifie

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Modifier upcharges (upcharge_basis attribute) generate incremental revenue that must be posted to specific revenue GL accounts. Foodservice finance teams map modifier revenue to GL accounts for accura

Indicates whether any modifier options within this group may introduce allergen variations that require disclosure to guests. True = allergen-relevant group requiring allergen declaration logic at POS/OLO; False = no allergen impact. reference: FDA Menu Labeling Rule (21 CFR Part 101.11); NRA Nutrition Labeling Standards

description: Defines which ordering channels (DT = Drive-Thru, OLO = Online Ordering, 3PD = Third-Party Delivery, POS = in-store Point of Sale) this modifier group is active on. Enables channel-specific menu configuration and supports omnichannel menu engineering. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|pos_only|olo_only|dt_only|3pd_only|pos_olo|pos_dt — promote to reference product]

description: The business code of the modifier option that is pre-selected by default when a guest views this modifier group on POS or OLO. Supports speed of service (SOS) optimization by pre-populating the most common selection. Null if no default applies.
