description: Customer-facing label shown on POS screens, KDS (Kitchen Display System), OLO (Online Ordering) interfaces, and 3PD (Third-Party Delivery) menus. May differ from the internal group_name for marketing or localization purposes. Example: 'Choose Your Protein', 'Pick a Sauce'.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; NRA ServSafe Standards

description: Numeric ordering position that controls the sequence in which this modifier group is presented to the guest on POS screens, KDS, OLO, and 3PD interfaces. Lower values appear first. Enables menu engineers to control the customization flow and upsell sequencing.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: The date after which this modifier group configuration is no longer valid. Null for open-ended configurations. Supports scheduled menu deactivation, LTO (Limited Time Offer) lifecycle management, and menu version control.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: The date from which this modifier group configuration is considered active and valid for use in POS, OLO, and 3PD channels. Supports menu lifecycle management, scheduled menu changes, and NRO (New Restaurant Opening) menu activation.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Number of modifier selections within this group that are included at no additional charge. Selections beyond this limit may incur an upcharge per the individual modifier's pricing. A value of 0 means all selections are charged; null means unlimited free selections. Supports COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) management and pricing strategy.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; SAP S/4HANA — CO (Controlling)

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the modifier group, used in POS configuration, OLO platform integration, and 3PD channel mapping. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Example: 'PROT-CHOICE', 'SAUCE-SEL', 'SIZE-UPG'.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Internal business name for the modifier group used in back-office systems, menu engineering reports, and POS configuration. Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Example: 'Protein Choice', 'Sauce Selection', 'Size Upgrade'.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module

description: Categorical classification of the modifier group by its culinary or operational function. Drives menu engineering analysis (PMIX), reporting by customization category, and POS screen layout. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: protein|sauce|size|add_on|side|beverage|preparation|topping|crust|dietary — promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

description: URL pointing to the visual asset (image) representing this modifier group on digital ordering platforms (OLO, kiosk, 3PD). Used to enhance the guest customization experience on digital channels. Null if no image is assigned.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Digital Asset Management

description: Indicates whether this modifier group can be collapsed or hidden in the guest-facing UI when not interacted with. True = group can be collapsed to reduce screen clutter on OLO and kiosk interfaces; False = always fully expanded. Supports UX optimization for digital ordering channels.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — UI Configuration

description: Indicates whether franchise operators are permitted to modify the configuration of this modifier group (e.g., adjust min/max selections, add local modifiers) within the bounds of the franchise agreement. True = franchisee may configure; False = corporate-locked configuration. Supports franchise management and brand standards compliance.
reference: FranConnect — Franchise Compliance Tracking; IFA Franchise Best Practices; FTC Franchise Disclosure Regulations

description: Indicates whether this modifier group and its selected options are displayed on the KDS (Kitchen Display System) in the BOH (Back of House). True = modifier group selections are shown to kitchen staff; False = suppressed from KDS display (e.g., packaging or FOH-only modifiers). Supports BOH operational efficiency.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — KDS Integration; NRA SOP Standards

description: Indicates whether this modifier group is associated with a Limited Time Offer (LTO) promotional campaign. True = LTO modifier group with defined start/end dates; False = permanent menu modifier group. Supports marketing campaign execution and menu lifecycle management.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Salesforce CRM — Marketing Automation

description: Indicates whether the modifier group's selected options are printed on the guest receipt. True = selections appear on receipt for guest reference; False = suppressed from receipt printing. Supports guest transparency and order accuracy.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Receipt Configuration; FDA Food Labeling Regulations

description: Indicates whether the guest must make at least one selection from this modifier group before the parent menu item can be added to the order. True = mandatory selection; False = optional. Derived operationally from min_selections > 0 but stored explicitly for POS/OLO rendering logic.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Reference key used to look up translated display names and descriptions for this modifier group in multi-language markets. Supports international franchise operations and localized guest-facing menu presentation across global markets.

description: The date on which this modifier group is deactivated as part of a Limited Time Offer (LTO) promotion. Null for permanent modifier groups. Used by POS and OLO systems for automated menu deactivation scheduling.

description: The date on which this modifier group becomes available as part of a Limited Time Offer (LTO) promotion. Null for permanent modifier groups. Used by POS and OLO systems for automated menu activation scheduling.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Salesforce CRM — Marketing Automation

description: Maximum number of modifier options a guest may select from this group. For single-select groups this is typically 1. For multi-select groups this caps the number of add-ons or customizations. Enforced at POS and OLO order validation.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Minimum number of modifier options a guest must select from this group to complete an order. A value of 0 indicates the group is optional. A value of 1 or more makes the group mandatory. Enforced at POS and OLO order validation.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Detailed narrative description of the modifier group's purpose, scope, and applicable customization context. Used by menu engineers and franchise operators for documentation and training materials.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module

description: Current lifecycle state of the modifier group. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. 'active' = currently in use on POS/OLO; 'inactive' = temporarily disabled; 'draft' = under menu engineering review; 'archived' = retired from all channels; 'suspended' = paused due to supply or compliance issue.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Free-text field for operational notes, menu engineering commentary, or franchise-specific instructions related to this modifier group. Used by menu engineers, franchise business consultants, and operations teams for context that does not fit structured fields.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module

description: Native identifier assigned to this modifier group within the Olo Digital Ordering Platform. Used for synchronization between the enterprise menu master and the OLO channel, ensuring consistent customization options across in-store and digital ordering.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Menu Management

description: Indicates whether modifier selections within this group are tracked in PMIX (Product Mix) reporting for menu engineering analysis. True = modifier selection data flows into PMIX analytics; False = excluded from PMIX reporting (e.g., administrative or packaging modifiers). Supports menu engineering and revenue management decisions.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Reporting Module; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

business_glossary_term: Point of Sale (POS) Modifier Group Reference
description: Native identifier or reference code assigned to this modifier group within the Oracle MICROS POS system. Used for reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the POS system of record during menu synchronization and audit processes.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module

description: Specifies which restaurant format(s) — QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant), casual dining, or fine dining — this modifier group applies to. Supports multi-format menu governance across the enterprise's diverse restaurant portfolio.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; NRA Industry Standards

description: Defines whether a guest may select only one modifier (single-select, e.g., Size Upgrade) or multiple modifiers (multi-select, e.g., Add-Ons, Sauce Selection) from this group. Drives POS and OLO customization logic and guest-facing UI rendering.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Defines how individual modifier options within this group are sorted when displayed to the guest. 'manual' = display_sequence on each modifier; 'alphabetical' = A-Z by modifier name; 'price_asc/desc' = by price; 'popularity' = by historical selection frequency from PMIX data. Supports menu engineering and upsell optimization.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this modifier group record originated. Supports data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and master data management in the lakehouse silver layer. reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Olo Digital Ordering Platform; FranConnect; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage Standards

description: Defines how additional charges are applied when a guest selects modifiers beyond the free_modifier_limit. 'per_item' = each additional modifier is charged individually; 'flat_group' = a single flat charge applies to the group regardless of count; 'none' = no upcharges apply. Drives POS pricing logic and revenue management.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; SAP S/4HANA — SD (Sales and Distribution)

description: The date and time when this modifier group record was last modified in the system of record. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Requirements

description: Monotonically incrementing version counter for this modifier group record. Incremented each time the group configuration is updated. Supports menu change management, audit trails, and rollback capabilities in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management Module; SAP S/4HANA — Change Management

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each 86 event record — when a menu item is temporarily pulled from service at a restaurant. Primary key of this table.

Foreign key linking to order.channel. Business justification: When an item is 86'd, the specific channel affected (OLO, drive-thru, dine-in) must be recorded for channel suppression actions and impact reporting. channel_affected is a denormalized plain column; r

Foreign key linking to foodsafety.inspection_violation. Business justification: When a health inspector orders removal of a menu item due to a violation, the 86 event must be traceable to the triggering inspection_violation for compliance documentation and corrective action track

Foreign key linking to realestate.maintenance_work_order. Business justification: Equipment failure causing item unavailability (86 event) must be correlated with the maintenance work order raised to resolve it. QSR operations teams use this link for root cause analysis, SLA compli

Reference to the specific kitchen equipment unit that failed and caused the 86 event, when reason_code = 'equipment_failure'. Supports Repairs and Maintenance (R&M) tracking and equipment reliability analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — Fixed Assets (FA); Oracle MICROS POS — Kitchen Equipment

description: Reference to the employee (Back of House (BOH) or Front of House (FOH) staff member) who first reported or logged the 86 event. Links to workforce employee master.
reference: Workday HCM — Workforce Management; OSHA Incident Reporting Standards

description: Reference to the menu item that was 86'd (removed from service). Links to the menu.menu_item master record.

Foreign key linking to supply.recall_event. Business justification: FDA/USDA recall traceability: when is_recall_related=true on an 86 event, regulators and operations teams require a direct link to the specific recall_event for compliance reporting, affected-location

Reference to the restaurant location where the 86 event occurred. Enables operational and geographic analysis of item unavailability.

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: An 86 event is fundamentally triggered by stock depletion of a specific stock_item. This FK enables inventory-driven 86 management, post-event root cause analysis, and automated reorder triggers. The
Reference to the restaurant location where the 86 event occurred. Enables operational and geographic analysis of item unavailability.
Date and time when this 86 event record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Standards; PCI DSS Record Integrity

description: The daypart during which the 86 event was active or first occurred. Enables PMIX (Product Mix) and daypart-level impact analysis. Aligns with menu.daypart definitions.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Daypart Configuration; NRA Daypart Standards

description: Total elapsed time in minutes from start_timestamp to end_timestamp. Populated upon resolution. Used for Speed of Service (SOS) impact analysis and operational KPI reporting.
Date and time when the menu item was restored to service and the 86 event was closed. Null if the event is still open. Used to calculate total downtime duration.
The internal team to which the 86 event was escalated, if applicable. Supports accountability tracking and cross-functional response coordination.
reference: Zenput — Operational Compliance; FranConnect — Franchise Compliance

description: Date and time when the 86 event was escalated to operations management, supply chain, or franchise support teams. Null if the event was resolved without escalation. Supports SLA and response time analysis.
reference: Zenput — Operational Compliance; FranConnect — Franchise Compliance

description: Estimated number of guest covers (transactions) that could not be fulfilled due to the 86 event during the affected period. Derived operationally at the restaurant level for guest experience impact reporting. Not a calculated KPI — captured as a manager estimate at time of resolution.
reference: NRA — Guest Experience Standards; Oracle MICROS POS — Transaction Reporting

description: Human-readable, externally referenceable identifier for the 86 event (e.g., 86-2024-07-15-A3F9K2). Used in operational communications, Zenput audit tasks, and guest experience escalations.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management; Zenput — Operational Compliance

description: Current lifecycle state of the 86 event. 'open' = item currently unavailable; 'resolved' = item restored to service; 'escalated' = issue elevated to operations or supply chain team; 'cancelled' = event logged in error.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management; NRA SOP Standards

description: Indicates whether a guest-facing notification was sent (e.g., via OLO or 3PD platform) to inform customers that the item is temporarily unavailable. True = notification dispatched. Supports Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) and Net Promoter Score (NPS) impact tracking.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Guest Notifications; NRA Guest Experience Standards

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Critical Control Point (CCP) Reference
description: Reference code to the specific HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) that was breached or triggered this 86 event, if applicable. Used for food safety audit trails and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: HACCP — Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; ISO 22000

description: Indicates whether the 86 event was triggered by a food safety concern (e.g., HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) failure, quality issue, or regulatory recall). True = food safety event requiring Zenput audit task and potential regulatory notification.
reference: HACCP — Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Indicates whether the 86'd item is a Limited Time Offer (LTO). True = LTO item. 86 events on LTO items carry heightened business impact due to marketing commitments and guest expectations, and may require immediate escalation.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Management; NRA LTO Best Practices

description: Indicates whether the 86 event was triggered by a formal supplier or regulatory product recall. True = item must not be served and recall protocol must be followed per FDA/USDA guidelines.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; USDA Meat and Poultry Inspection; ISO 22000

business_glossary_term: Kitchen Display System (KDS) Alert Sent Flag
description: Indicates whether a Kitchen Display System (KDS) alert was sent to BOH staff notifying them of the 86 event. True = KDS alert dispatched. Supports BOH operational awareness and throughput management.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Kitchen Display System (KDS)

description: The item identifier in the Olo Digital Ordering Platform at the time of the 86 event. Used to confirm whether the item was also suppressed on the OLO channel during the event.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Menu Management

description: Indicates whether the item was suppressed on the Olo Digital Ordering Platform during the 86 event. True = item hidden from online ordering menu. Prevents guest orders for unavailable items on digital channels.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform — Menu Management

description: Indicates whether the restaurant where the 86 event occurred is a franchise location or a company-owned location. Drives escalation routing and compliance accountability.
reference: FranConnect — Franchise Compliance; IFA Franchise Best Practices

description: The Point of Sale (POS) item code as configured in Oracle MICROS POS at the time of the 86 event. Captured at event time to preserve the POS state even if the menu item code changes later.

business_glossary_term: Point of Sale (POS) Item Suppressed Flag
description: Indicates whether the item was actively suppressed (hidden/disabled) on the Oracle MICROS POS system during the 86 event. True = item removed from POS ordering screens. Ensures operational accuracy and prevents staff from ringing up unavailable items.

Standardized reason code explaining why the menu item was removed from service. 'out_of_stock' = inventory depleted; 'equipment_failure' = kitchen equipment down; 'quality_issue' = item failed quality/HACCP check; 'recall' = supplier or regulatory recall; 'supplier_delay' = ingredient not delivered; 'prep_time_exceeded' = throughput constraint. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: out_of_stock|equipment_failure|quality_issue|recall|supplier_delay|prep_time_exceeded — promote to reference product]
reference: HACCP — Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; NRA SOP Standards

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context beyond the reason_code. For example, specifying which piece of equipment failed, which ingredient was out of stock, or the nature of the quality issue. Supports root cause analysis.
reference: Zenput — Operational Compliance; NRA SOP Standards

description: Job role of the employee who reported the 86 event (e.g., manager, Back of House (BOH) staff, Front of House (FOH) staff, shift lead, franchisee). Supports accountability and training gap analysis.
reference: Workday HCM — Job Role Classification; NRA ServSafe Certification

description: The corrective action taken to resolve the 86 event and restore the item to service. 'restocked' = inventory replenished; 'equipment_repaired' = kitchen equipment fixed; 'item_substituted' = alternative item offered; 'recalled_item_removed' = item permanently pulled per recall; 'supplier_order_placed' = emergency procurement initiated; 'no_action' = event closed without resolution (e.g., end of daypart). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: restocked|equipment_repaired|item_substituted|recalled_item_removed|supplier_order_placed|no_action — promote to reference product]
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management; Zenput — Operational Compliance; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act

description: The restaurant format (Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual, or fine-dining) of the location where the 86 event occurred. Supports format-level operational benchmarking.
reference: FranConnect — Franchise Development; NRA Restaurant Classification Standards

description: The operational system of record from which this 86 event was originated or captured. 'micros_pos' = Oracle MICROS POS; 'zenput' = Zenput operational compliance; 'marketman' = MarketMan inventory alert; 'olo' = Olo platform suppression event; 'manual' = manually entered by operations staff.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS; Zenput; MarketMan Inventory Management; Olo Digital Ordering Platform

description: Date and time when the menu item was first removed from service (86'd). This is the principal real-world event timestamp. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.

Date and time when this 86 event record was last modified in the data platform, such as when the event was resolved, escalated, or corrected. Supports data lineage and change tracking in the Silver layer.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Audit Trail Standards; PCI DSS Record Integrity

description: Reference to the Zenput food safety audit task or operational compliance task created in response to this 86 event. Enables traceability between the 86 event and the corrective action workflow in Zenput.
reference: Zenput — Food Safety Audits and Operational Compliance

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each item cost record. Primary key for the item_cost data product in the menu domain.

Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Item cost approval is a financial control process requiring a named employee (e.g., finance controller or menu engineering manager) for SOX compliance and food cost variance reporting in restaurant op

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost accounting assigns each menu item's calculated cost to a cost center for internal budgeting and variance analysis; essential for COGS reporting.

Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: item_cost has a plain-text 'fiscal_period' column (denormalized). Replacing with a proper FK to financial_period enables period-over-period food cost variance reporting (theoretical vs. actual COGS),

Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee COGS benchmarking is a named franchise compliance process — franchisors compare theoretical vs actual item cost by franchisee to identify food cost deviations and trigger corrective action.

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: COGS posting requires mapping item cost records to specific GL accounts (raw material cost, packaging cost). Foodservice finance teams post theoretical and actual COGS to GL accounts for P&L reporting
Reference to the menu item for which this theoretical cost record is calculated. Links to the menu.menu_item master product.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center-level food cost variance reporting is a standard foodservice finance process — each restaurant unit maps to a profit center, and COGS must be attributed to profit centers for unit-level
Reference to the specific recipe version (Bill of Materials) used to derive the theoretical cost. Ensures cost traceability to the exact ingredient composition at time of calculation.

business_glossary_term: Actual Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (Actual CoGS%)
description: Actual realized Cost of Goods Sold percentage sourced from MarketMan Inventory Management, reflecting real purchase costs, waste, and yield losses. Compared against theoretical_cogs_pct to compute variance and identify operational inefficiencies.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management — Actual COGS Report; GAAP / IFRS

description: The date on which this item cost record was approved by the authorized reviewer. Used for audit trail and to establish the effective start of this cost record in menu engineering and P&L reporting.
reference: GAAP / IFRS — Internal Controls; SOX — Financial Reporting Controls

description: The menu item's base selling price at the time of cost calculation, used as the denominator for CoGS% computation. Captured as a point-in-time snapshot to ensure historical cost percentage accuracy even when prices change.
reference: Oracle MICROS POS — Menu Price Management; GAAP / IFRS

description: The sales channel for which this cost record applies: dine-in, Drive-Thru (DT), Online Ordering (OLO), Third-Party Delivery (3PD), or all channels. Packaging and portion costs may differ by channel, resulting in channel-specific cost records.
reference: Olo Digital Ordering Platform; Oracle MICROS POS — Channel Management

business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold Percentage Variance (CoGS% Variance)
description: Difference between actual_cogs_pct and theoretical_cogs_pct (actual minus theoretical). Positive variance indicates actual costs exceeded theoretical expectations, signaling waste, theft, or pricing issues. Critical metric for P&L reporting and operational control.
reference: GAAP / IFRS — Variance Analysis; NRA Food Cost Management Standards

description: The business date on which the theoretical item cost was calculated. Used to track cost trends over time and align with menu engineering review cycles and P&L reporting periods.

Method used to derive the item cost: 'theoretical_bom' = derived from recipe Bill of Materials and price snapshot; 'actual_marktman' = sourced directly from MarketMan actual cost data; 'blended' = weighted combination of theoretical and actual. Ensures analytical transparency.
reference: MarketMan Inventory Management — Cost Calculation; Internal COGS Methodology SOP

description: Theoretical cost per gram of the menu item (theoretical_cost_amount / portion_size_grams). Enables normalized cost comparison across items of different portion sizes and supports portion engineering decisions.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering SOP; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

description: Lifecycle status of this item cost record. 'draft' indicates pending review; 'approved' indicates validated and active for menu engineering; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer calculation; 'archived' indicates retired from active use.
The timestamp when this item cost record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit creation trail for the Silver layer record.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GAAP / IFRS — Audit Trail Requirements

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the cost amounts are expressed (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across international franchise markets.

The daypart for which this cost record applies (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night, or all). Ingredient compositions and portion standards may vary by daypart, resulting in daypart-specific cost records for the same menu item.
reference: Internal Menu Engineering SOP; Oracle MICROS POS — Daypart Configuration

description: The date on which this item cost record ceases to be active, typically when superseded by a new calculation. Null indicates the record is currently active. Supports bi-temporal cost history and trend analysis.
reference: GAAP / IFRS — Period Cost Recognition; Internal Menu Engineering SOP

description: The date from which this item cost record is considered active and applicable for menu engineering, pricing, and P&L reporting. Supports bi-temporal modeling of cost history.

description: Total number of distinct ingredients in the Bill of Materials (BOM) used for this cost calculation. Provides a quick indicator of recipe complexity and the number of price inputs contributing to the theoretical cost.

reference: Internal LTO Management SOP; Oracle MICROS POS — LTO Configuration

description: The native record identifier from MarketMan Inventory Management for the actual cost data used in this calculation. Enables direct traceability back to the source system record for audit and reconciliation purposes.

description: Menu engineering quadrant classification based on the item's profitability (CoGS%) and popularity (PMIX). 'star' = high popularity, high margin; 'plow_horse' = high popularity, low margin; 'puzzle' = low popularity, high margin; 'dog' = low popularity, low margin. Drives pricing and promotional strategy.
reference: NRA — Menu Engineering Framework (Kasavana & Smith); Internal Menu Engineering SOP

description: Free-text notes from the menu engineering or finance team explaining the rationale for this cost calculation, any manual adjustments made, or context for significant variances. Supports audit review and knowledge transfer.

description: Standard portion size in grams for the menu item as defined in the recipe BOM at the time of cost calculation. Used to normalize cost-per-gram comparisons across menu items and to validate portion compliance with HACCP standards.

description: The pricing basis applied to ingredient costs in this calculation: 'last_purchase' = most recent purchase price; 'moving_average' = weighted average of recent purchases; 'contract' = contracted supplier price from Coupa; 'standard' = standard cost set by finance. Affects cost comparability.

description: The effective date of the ingredient purchase prices used in this cost calculation. Distinct from cost_calculation_date — the snapshot may use prices from a prior period (e.g., last purchase price, rolling average). Critical for cost audit and variance explanation.

description: Reference to the ingredient price snapshot used in this cost calculation. Captures the specific point-in-time purchase prices from MarketMan that were applied to the Bill of Materials to derive theoretical Cost of Goods Sold (COGS).

description: Cost contribution of the primary protein ingredient(s) to the total theoretical item cost. Proteins (beef, chicken, seafood) are typically the highest-cost BOM component and are tracked separately for commodity price sensitivity analysis and USDA-aligned procurement reporting.

description: Version identifier of the recipe / Bill of Materials (BOM) used in this cost calculation (e.g., 'v3.2'). Enables traceability of cost changes driven by recipe reformulations versus ingredient price movements.

description: The restaurant format for which this cost record applies: Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual dining, fine dining, or all formats. Cost structures differ by format due to labor, portion, and ingredient standards.

description: The operational system of record from which this cost record originated: MarketMan (actual cost data), SAP S/4HANA (standard cost), Oracle MICROS POS (price data), or manual entry. Supports data lineage and audit traceability in the Databricks Silver layer.

business_glossary_term: Target Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (Target CoGS%)
description: The budgeted or target CoGS% for this menu item as set by the finance and menu engineering teams. Used as the benchmark against which theoretical_cogs_pct and actual_cogs_pct are evaluated. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2800 = 28.00%).

description: Acceptable tolerance band for CoGS% variance (theoretical vs actual) before an alert or review is triggered. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0200 = ±2.00%). Supports automated exception reporting in menu engineering workflows.

business_glossary_term: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold Percentage (CoGS%)
description: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold expressed as a percentage of the menu item's base selling price (theoretical_cost_amount / base_price). Key menu engineering metric used to classify items and set pricing strategy. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2850 = 28.50%).
reference: NRA Menu Engineering Standards; GAAP / IFRS — Gross Margin Reporting

business_glossary_term: Theoretical Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Amount
description: The theoretical food cost amount in the operating currency for one unit of the menu item, derived by multiplying each Bill of Materials (BOM) ingredient quantity by its point-in-time purchase price. Core input to menu engineering and pricing decisions.
reference: GAAP / IFRS — Cost of Goods Sold; NRA Menu Engineering Standards

description: Absolute monetary variance between actual cost and theoretical cost per unit (actual cost amount minus theoretical_cost_amount). Expressed in the operating currency. Used alongside cogs_pct_variance for financial impact quantification in P&L reporting.

The timestamp when this item cost record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks the most recent update for change management and audit trail purposes in the Databricks Silver layer.

description: Theoretical food waste percentage factored into the cost calculation, representing expected yield loss during preparation (e.g., trim waste, cooking shrinkage). Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 = 5.00%). Sourced from recipe yield standards in MarketMan.

description: Expected usable yield percentage of the primary ingredient after preparation (e.g., 0.8500 = 85% yield after trimming). Used in yield management calculations to adjust raw ingredient quantities in the BOM to achieve the target portion size.

description: Date on which this ingredient version expires or is superseded within the recipe Bill of Materials (BOM). Null indicates the ingredient is currently active with no planned end date. Supports LTO lifecycle management and recipe change control.

description: Date from which this ingredient version is active and valid within the recipe Bill of Materials (BOM). Supports recipe versioning, Limited Time Offer (LTO) ingredient management, and historical COGS analysis.

description: Indicates whether this ingredient represents a Hazard Analysis Critical Control Point (HACCP) in the recipe preparation process requiring mandatory temperature, time, or handling controls. True = CCP applies; False = standard handling. Drives food safety audit requirements in Zenput.

description: Indicates whether this ingredient is designated as a critical or signature component of the recipe whose absence or substitution would materially alter the menu item's identity, quality standard, or brand promise. description: Indicates whether this ingredient holds a valid Halal certification from an accredited certifying body. True = Halal certified; False = not certified. Required for menu compliance in markets with Halal dietary requirements.

description: Indicates whether this ingredient holds a valid Kosher certification. True = Kosher certified; False = not certified. Required for menu compliance in markets with Kosher dietary requirements.
reference: Orthodox Union (OU) Kosher Certification; Internal Dietary Compliance Policy

description: Indicates whether this ingredient is certified organic under USDA National Organic Program (NOP) standards. True = USDA Organic certified; False = conventional. Supports menu labeling claims and premium ingredient sourcing tracking.

description: Indicates whether this ingredient may be substituted with an approved alternative ingredient without altering the recipe's quality, taste profile, or regulatory compliance. True = substitution permitted; False = ingredient is mandatory and cannot be substituted.
reference: Internal Recipe Governance Policy; FDA Food Labeling Regulations

description: Minimum required internal cooking temperature in degrees Fahrenheit for this ingredient as mandated by FDA Food Code and HACCP plan. Applicable when haccp_critical_control_point is True. Enforced via Zenput food safety task management.

description: Preparation state of the ingredient at the point of use in the recipe (e.g., raw, cooked, prepped, frozen, thawed). description: Maximum number of days this ingredient remains safe and usable from the date of receipt or preparation, under specified storage conditions. Used for PAR level management, waste reduction, and FIFO inventory rotation in MarketMan.
reference: FDA Food Code; MarketMan Inventory Management — Shelf Life Tracking; HACCP

description: Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Fahrenheit for this ingredient. Exceeding this threshold triggers a food safety violation per HACCP plan and FDA Food Code requirements.
Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Fahrenheit for this ingredient to maintain food safety and quality standards. Used in HACCP temperature monitoring and cold chain compliance tracking.

description: The fiscal year for which the budget_amount is applicable. Enables multi-year budget tracking and year-over-year variance analysis.
The SAP business area classification. Used to segment financial reporting by line of business (e.g., QSR, casual dining, catering, franchise operations) for multi-brand or multi-format portfolios.

Indicates whether this cost center is eligible to receive capital expenditure allocations. True for restaurant units (equipment, R&M), distribution centers, and major corporate facilities. False for pure administrative departments. Used to distinguish CapEx from OpEx in financial reporting.

business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold Percent (COGS%) Target
description: The target COGS% (cost of goods sold as a percentage of revenue) for this cost center. Critical KPI for food cost management. Typical QSR targets range from 25-35%. Used for menu engineering, pricing strategy, and supplier negotiations.

The SAP Controlling Area to which this cost center belongs. Controlling areas represent the highest level of cost accounting organization, typically aligned with legal entities or major geographic regions for multi-entity consolidation.

High-level categorization of the cost center's role. Revenue-generating centers (FOH, BOH) directly contribute to sales; support centers (maintenance, training) enable operations; administrative centers (HR, finance) provide governance; overhead centers (facilities, utilities) are indirect; capital project centers track CapEx initiatives.
The externally-known alphanumeric code for the cost center as defined in SAP S/4HANA Controlling module. Used in financial reporting and P&L statements.
Free-text description providing additional context about this cost center's purpose, scope, or special characteristics. Used for documentation and reporting clarity.
The full business name of the cost center (e.g., 'Store #1234 - Downtown Chicago', 'Regional Office - Midwest', 'Corporate HR Department').

Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active centers accept cost postings; inactive centers are temporarily disabled; pending centers are awaiting approval; closed centers are permanently retired; suspended centers are on hold; planned centers are scheduled for future activation (e.g., NRO pipeline).

Classification of the cost center by organizational function. Restaurant units are individual QSR locations; regional offices manage geographic territories; corporate departments are headquarters functions; shared service centers provide centralized support; distribution centers handle supply chain; franchise support manages franchisee relations.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where this cost center operates. Used for multi-country consolidation, currency translation, and jurisdiction-specific compliance (FDA, USDA, local health departments).
The user ID of the person who created this cost center record. Used for audit trails and accountability.
The timestamp when this cost center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for this cost center's local currency. Used for budget planning, cost allocation, and multi-currency consolidation in GAAP/IFRS financial statements.
The number of drive-thru lanes at this location. Nullable for non-DT formats. Critical for SOS (Speed of Service) analysis, throughput capacity planning, and DT-specific cost allocation.

The restaurant format classification. Examples: DT (Drive-Thru), DINE_IN, KIOSK, FOOD_COURT, AIRPORT, GHOST_KITCHEN. Used for format-specific cost benchmarking, throughput analysis, and real estate site selection.

Indicates whether this cost center represents a franchise-owned unit (true) or a company-owned unit (false). Critical for revenue recognition (royalty income vs direct sales), P&L consolidation, and franchise compliance tracking.
The functional area classification for cross-company-code reporting. Typical values include operations, marketing, R&D, administration, IT, finance. Used for functional P&L views and OpEx analysis.

The target Labor% (labor cost as a percentage of revenue) for this cost center. Critical KPI for restaurant operations. Typical QSR targets range from 20-30%. Used for workforce scheduling optimization and P&L performance evaluation.
The user ID of the person who last modified this cost center record. Used for audit trails and change accountability.
The timestamp when this cost center record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data freshness validation.
Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or historical context. Examples: 'Undergoing renovation Q2 2024', 'Pilot location for new POS system', 'Seasonal closure Nov-Mar'.

The method used to allocate shared operating expenses to this cost center. Direct costs are posted directly; allocated methods distribute corporate overhead based on revenue, headcount, square footage, transaction volume, or fixed percentages. Used for full-absorption P&L reporting.
The cost center code of the immediate parent in the organizational hierarchy. Nullable for top-level cost centers. Enables recursive hierarchy traversal for regional and corporate rollups.
The geographic region code for this cost center. Used for regional P&L rollups, SSS (Same-Store Sales) analysis, and regional manager accountability. Examples: NEAST, SWEST, MIDWEST, INTL.

The number of customer seats available at this cost center location. Nullable for non-dine-in formats (drive-thru only, ghost kitchens). Used for cover count analysis, table turn calculations, and throughput benchmarking.
The total interior square footage of this cost center facility. Used for OpEx allocation (utilities, CAM charges), productivity metrics (sales per square foot), and real estate valuation.

The date from which this cost center becomes effective and can accept cost postings. Aligns with NRO (New Restaurant Opening) dates for restaurant units or organizational change effective dates for corporate centers.
The date through which this cost center remains effective. Nullable for open-ended cost centers. Populated when a restaurant closes, a department is dissolved, or a regional office is consolidated.
Unique identifier for the financial period record. Primary key.
Foreign key reference to the immediately preceding period (e.g., prior month). Used for sequential period-over-period analysis.
Foreign key reference to the equivalent period in the prior fiscal year. Used for year-over-year comparative analysis and SSS reporting.
Self-referencing FK on financial_period (prior_financial_period_id)

Accounting standard applied in this ledger: US_GAAP (United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards), local_GAAP (country-specific standards), management (internal reporting).
Indicates whether adjustment postings (corrections, reclassifications) are allowed in this period after initial close. Used for controlled reopening during audit or correction cycles.
Reason code or description for why the period is an adjustment period.
Indicates whether this period is subject to external audit review. Typically true for year-end periods and quarterly periods for publicly traded entities.
True if changes to this period are tracked for audit purposes.
Total number of calendar days in the period. Used for daily average calculations and period normalization in financial analysis.
Month number (1‑12) in the calendar year.
Quarter number (1‑4) in the calendar year.
Gregorian calendar year of the period.
Actual date when the period-end close process was completed and the period status changed to closed. Null if period is still open.
Target date for completing the period-end close process. Used for close calendar planning and tracking adherence to financial reporting deadlines.
Number of days between scheduled and actual close date (positive if late, negative if early). Used to track period-end close efficiency and identify process improvement opportunities.
Four-character alphanumeric code identifying the legal entity in SAP S/4HANA. Links this period to a specific company within the multi-entity structure.
Code identifying the consolidation group for multi‑entity reporting.
Indicates whether this period requires multi-entity consolidation processing. True for periods where intercompany eliminations and group reporting are performed.
User ID or username of the person who created this financial period record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Timestamp when this financial period record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Total count of calendar days covered by the period.
Date when the period ceases to be effective (nullable).
Date when the period becomes effective for reporting purposes.
Last calendar day of the financial period.
Conversion rate from functional currency to reporting currency for the period.

Type of exchange rate applied for currency translation in this period: average (period average rate for P&L), closing (period-end rate for balance sheet), budget (planning rate), historical (acquisition date rate for fixed assets).
Optional free‑text description of the period.
Type of financial statement the period primarily supports.
Month number (1‑12) within the fiscal year.
Standard code used by SAP for the fiscal period (e.g., "2023P01").
Quarter number (1‑4) within the fiscal year.
Four-digit fiscal year to which this period belongs. May differ from calendar year depending on fiscal year variant configuration.

Two-character code defining the fiscal calendar structure (number of periods, special periods, year-end rules) used by the company code. Determines how the fiscal year is divided into posting periods.
Degree to which holidays affect the period.
True if the period is used for post‑close adjustments.
True if this period is the active reporting period.
True if the period values are based on estimates rather than actuals.
True if the period includes a recognized holiday schedule.
True if the period is an interim (non‑full) reporting period.
Classification of the ledger: leading (primary GAAP ledger for statutory reporting), non_leading (parallel IFRS or local GAAP ledger), special_purpose (management or segment reporting ledger).
True when the period is locked for posting to prevent changes.
User ID or username of the person who last modified this financial period record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Timestamp when this financial period record was last modified. Updated whenever period status, close dates, or other attributes change.
Free-text field for recording period-specific notes, exceptions, or special circumstances (e.g., 'Includes acquisition of XYZ brand', 'Impacted by COVID-19 closures', 'Leap year adjustment').
Business classification of the period data.
Last calendar date of the financial period. Defines the end of the posting window for transactions assigned to this period.
Compact code representing the period (e.g., FY2023Q1, FY2023M04).
Human-readable name or label for the period (e.g., 'January 2024', 'Q1 FY2024', 'Period 13 - Year-End Adjustments'). Used for reporting and user interface display.
Sequential period number within the fiscal year (typically 1-12 for monthly periods, or 1-13 for 4-4-5 retail calendars). Special periods (13, 14, 15, 16) used for year-end adjustments.
Sequential order of the period within its fiscal year.
First calendar date of the financial period. Defines the beginning of the posting window for transactions assigned to this period.

Current posting status of the period: not_opened (future period, no posting allowed), open (active posting allowed), closed (period-end close completed, limited posting), locked (archived, no posting allowed).
Classification of the period: regular (standard posting period), special (year-end adjustment period 13-16), or adjustment (mid-year correction period).
Indicates whether new transactions can be posted to this period. True if period is open for posting, false if closed or locked.
Fiscal quarter number (1-4) to which this period belongs. Supports quarterly financial reporting and Same-Store Sales (SSS) analysis.
reference: GAAP Financial Reporting, SEC Regulation S-X

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for consolidated financial reporting in this period (e.g., USD for US Dollar, EUR for Euro). Used for multi-currency consolidation.
True if the period is used for segment‑level reporting.
Originating system that defines the period (e.g., SAP S/4HANA).
Indicates whether this period is eligible for Same-Store Sales (SSS) or Comparable Store Sales (Comp Sales) analysis. Typically true for periods where stores have been open for at least 12-13 months.
First calendar day of the financial period.
True if the period is used for tax reporting calculations.
Indicates whether this period is a tax reporting period requiring submission of tax returns (e.g., quarterly estimated tax, annual tax filing). Used for tax compliance tracking.
reference: IRS Tax Code, Local Tax Regulations

description: Number of business working days in the period, excluding weekends and holidays. Used for normalizing operational metrics and labor cost analysis.
Concatenated key combining fiscal year and period number (format: YYYYPPP, e.g., 2024001 for FY2024 Period 1). Used for sorting and comparative period analysis.
Unique identifier for the general ledger account record. Primary key.
Reference to the legal entity (company code) to which this GL account belongs. Supports multi-entity consolidation across franchise and company-owned operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Company Code; GAAP/IFRS Consolidation Requirements

description: Reference to the parent account in the account hierarchy for roll-up reporting. Null for top-level accounts. Enables multi-level account group hierarchies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Account Hierarchy; GAAP/IFRS Financial Statement Aggregation

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for accounts that manage balances in a specific currency (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Null for accounts managed only in company code currency.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA Currency Management

description: Detailed business description of the account purpose, usage guidelines, and what types of transactions should be posted to this account.
SAP account group code that defines the account's characteristics, field status, and number range. Controls account behavior and validation rules in SAP S/4HANA.
Human-readable short name or title of the GL account (e.g., 'Food Cost - Beef', 'Franchise Royalty Revenue', 'Depreciation - Equipment').
The externally-known unique account code used across all legal entities in the chart of accounts. Typically 6-10 digit numeric code following SAP S/4HANA GL numbering convention.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Chart of Accounts; GAAP/IFRS Account Structure

description: Current lifecycle status of the GL account: 'active' for normal use, 'blocked' for temporarily suspended, 'closed' for permanently retired, 'pending_activation' for newly created accounts awaiting approval.
reference: SAP S/4HANA GL Master Data Lifecycle

description: High-level classification of the account into one of the five fundamental financial statement categories: asset, liability, equity, revenue, or expense.
Alternative or legacy account code used for cross-reference, migration mapping, or external reporting (e.g., mapping to previous ERP system account numbers).
reference: Data Migration Standards; Legacy System Integration

description: Specific balance sheet line item classification for asset, liability, and equity accounts. Set to 'not_applicable' for P&L (Profit and Loss) accounts (revenue/expense).

Classification of this account for cash flow statement presentation: 'operating' for operational cash flows, 'investing' for CapEx (Capital Expenditure) and asset transactions, 'financing' for debt and equity, 'not_applicable' for non-cash accounts.
Mapped account number used in corporate consolidation reporting to standardize accounts across multiple legal entities and chart of accounts structures.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Consolidation Standards (ASC 810 / IFRS 10); SAP S/4HANA Consolidation

description: Classification of cost element: 'primary' for costs posted from external transactions (FI to CO), 'secondary' for internal allocations and assessments, 'not_applicable' if not a cost element.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Element Categories

description: Flag indicating whether this GL account is also defined as a cost element in SAP Controlling (CO) module for cost center and internal order postings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling) Cost Element Master

description: User ID or name of the person who created this GL account record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA User Audit; SOX Compliance

description: Date and time when this GL account record was first created in the system. Audit trail for account master data governance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; SOX Compliance

description: SAP field status group code that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed when posting transactions to this account.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Field Status Variant Configuration

description: Detailed sub-classification within the account type for financial statement presentation (e.g., 'Current Assets', 'Long-Term Liabilities', 'Operating Revenue', 'Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)', 'Selling General and Administrative (SG&A)').
reference: GAAP/IFRS Financial Statement Presentation; SAP S/4HANA Financial Statement Version

description: Business function or operational area this account supports (e.g., 'Restaurant Operations', 'Franchise', 'Supply Chain', 'Marketing', 'Corporate', 'Real Estate'). Used for segment reporting and management accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Functional Area; GAAP/IFRS Segment Reporting (ASC 280 / IFRS 8)

business_glossary_term: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) Account Indicator
description: Flag indicating whether this account is used in GAAP-compliant financial statements. Supports dual-ledger reporting for companies reporting under both GAAP and IFRS.

business_glossary_term: International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) Account Indicator
description: Flag indicating whether this account is used in IFRS-compliant financial statements. Supports dual-ledger reporting for companies reporting under both GAAP and IFRS.
Flag indicating whether this account is used for intercompany transactions between legal entities that require elimination during consolidation.
User ID or name of the person who last modified this GL account record. Supports change tracking and accountability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA User Audit; SOX Compliance

description: Date and time when this GL account record was last updated. Tracks changes to account master data for audit and compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; SOX Compliance

description: Flag indicating whether individual line item details are stored and displayed for this account. True for most operational accounts; false for summary accounts.
Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or business context about this GL account (e.g., usage guidelines, approval requirements, historical context).
Flag indicating whether this account uses open item management for tracking and clearing individual transactions (e.g., for clearing accounts, suspense accounts).
Granularity level at which budget planning and forecasting is performed for this account (e.g., 'corporate', 'region', 'restaurant', 'cost_center').
reference: SAP S/4HANA Budget Planning; Management Accounting Best Practices

description: Flag indicating whether direct transaction postings are allowed to this account. False for summary/header accounts that only receive totals from sub-accounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA GL Account Master Field Status

description: Specific P&L statement line item classification for revenue and expense accounts (e.g., 'Sales Revenue', 'COGS (Cost of Goods Sold)', 'Labor Cost', 'Occupancy Cost', 'EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization)'). Null for balance sheet accounts.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Income Statement Structure; Restaurant Industry P&L Format

description: Flag indicating whether this is a reconciliation account (subledger control account) that summarizes detailed transactions from AP (Accounts Payable), AR (Accounts Receivable), or FA (Fixed Assets) subledgers.
Flag indicating whether transactions posted to this account are included in segment reporting for business unit, geographic, or product line analysis.
SAP sort key that determines the default sorting and grouping of line items posted to this account (e.g., by posting date, document number, or business partner).
reference: SAP S/4HANA Line Item Display Configuration

description: Flag indicating whether this is a statistical account used for tracking non-monetary quantities (e.g., headcount, transaction counts, cover counts) without affecting financial balances.
Tax treatment classification for this account (e.g., 'taxable_revenue', 'non_taxable_revenue', 'input_tax', 'output_tax', 'exempt'). Used for sales tax, VAT, and GST reporting.
reference: IRS Tax Code; State/Local Sales Tax Regulations; SAP S/4HANA Tax Configuration

description: Date from which this GL account becomes active and available for transaction posting. Supports time-dependent account master data.
Date until which this GL account remains active. Null for accounts with no planned end date. Used for account retirement and historical reporting.
Unique identifier for the journal entry record. Primary key for the journal entry header in SAP S/4HANA FI-GL.
SAP user ID of the person who approved this journal entry for posting. Populated only for entries requiring approval workflow. Used for segregation of duties compliance.
SAP user ID of the person who approved this journal entry for posting. Populated only for entries requiring approval workflow. Used for segregation of duties compliance.

Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Journal entries are posted within a specific financial period. The fiscal_period (INT) and fiscal_year (INT) columns are denormalized period identifiers that should be replaced by a FK to the financia

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Every GL journal entry in SAP FI-GL belongs to a specific company code (legal entity). Adding legal_entity_id normalizes the company_code string into a proper FK reference to the legal_entity master.
SAP user ID of the person or system account that posted this journal entry. Used for audit trail and segregation of duties monitoring.
reference: SOX Section 404 User Access Controls; SAP Security Standards

description: SAP user ID of the person who last modified this journal entry header (e.g., added notes, changed status). Blank if never modified after initial posting.
Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry was posted in a special adjustment period (periods 13-16) for year-end close. True if adjustment period; False if regular period.
Exact date and time when the journal entry was approved. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Null if no approval required or not yet approved.

Classification of the journal entry for audit sampling and risk assessment. Values: standard (routine posting), high_risk (unusual or large amount), manual_adjustment (manual journal entry requiring scrutiny), system_generated (automated posting).
reference: SOX Section 404 Risk Assessment; External Audit Standards

description: The baseline date from which payment terms are calculated (typically document date or posting date). Used to determine net due date and discount periods. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Name of the batch input session if this journal entry was posted via batch processing (e.g., mass upload, automated interface). Blank for manually posted entries.
The date on which this journal entry was cleared. Populated only for cleared documents. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
The document number of the clearing document if this journal entry has been cleared (e.g., open item cleared by payment or offsetting entry). Blank if not cleared.

Three-character code classifying the journal entry for consolidation purposes (e.g., 100=standard posting, 200=intercompany elimination, 300=currency translation adjustment, 400=consolidation adjustment). Used in multi-entity financial consolidation.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the document currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All line item amounts in this journal entry are denominated in this currency.
The date printed on the source document (invoice date, receipt date, contract date). May differ from posting date. Used for aging analysis and payment terms calculation. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Free-text description or memo entered at the document header level. Provides business context for the journal entry (e.g., 'Monthly depreciation run', 'Accrual for Q4 marketing expenses').

Ten-digit accounting document number assigned by SAP upon posting. The externally-known unique identifier for this journal entry within the company code and fiscal year. Used for audit trail and cross-reference.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Management; SOX Section 404 Internal Controls

description: Two-character code classifying the nature of the accounting document (e.g., SA=GL account document, KR=vendor invoice, DR=customer invoice, AB=accounting document, AA=asset posting). Controls number ranges and posting keys.
The calendar date on which the document was entered into the SAP system by the user. Used for audit trail and processing time analysis. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
The exchange rate applied to convert document currency to local currency at the time of posting. Expressed as local currency per one unit of document currency.
Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry involves intercompany transactions between multiple legal entities within the corporate group. True if intercompany; False otherwise.
Exact date and time of the last modification to this journal entry header. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Null if never modified.

Two-character code identifying the ledger in which this entry is posted (e.g., 0L=leading ledger for GAAP, 2L=IFRS ledger, 3L=management ledger). Supports parallel accounting for multiple reporting standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA New GL; GAAP/IFRS Parallel Accounting

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the company code local currency. Used for statutory reporting and consolidation.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP/IFRS Functional Currency

description: The calculated due date for payment based on payment terms and baseline date. Used for cash flow forecasting and AP/AR aging. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is in parked status (saved but not yet posted). True if parked; False if posted. Parked documents do not update GL balances until posted.
Four-character code defining payment terms for vendor invoices or customer invoices (e.g., net 30, 2/10 net 30). Used to calculate due dates and cash discount eligibility.
The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period assignment and is the primary date for financial reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA Posting Logic

description: Two-digit numeric code that controls the type of posting (debit or credit) and the account type (e.g., 40=debit GL, 50=credit GL, 01=debit customer, 31=debit vendor). Determines field status and processing logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Key Configuration

description: Exact date and time when the journal entry was posted to the general ledger. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and real-time reporting.
Free-text field for external reference numbers such as vendor invoice number, purchase order number, or intercompany document reference. Used for reconciliation and audit trail.
Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a prior posting. True if this document reverses another document; False otherwise.
Two-character code explaining why the document was reversed (e.g., 01=reversal in current period, 02=reversal in next period, 03=error correction). Populated only for reversal documents.
The document number of the original journal entry that this entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True. Maintains audit trail for accrual reversals and error corrections.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Reversal Logic; SOX Section 404

description: Code identifying the originating system for this journal entry (e.g., SAP_ECC, SAP_S4, LEGACY_GL, COUPA, WORKDAY). Used for data lineage and interface reconciliation.
The date used for tax reporting purposes (may differ from posting date based on jurisdiction rules). Used for VAT/GST returns and sales tax filings. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Four-character company code of the intercompany trading partner. Populated only when intercompany_indicator is True. Used for intercompany reconciliation and elimination entries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Intercompany Accounting; GAAP/IFRS Consolidation

description: SAP transaction code (T-code) used to create this journal entry (e.g., FB01, FB50, F-02). Indicates the posting method and user interface path.

Current state of the journal entry in the approval workflow. Values: draft (initial entry), pending_approval (submitted for review), approved (authorized but not posted), posted (final state in GL), rejected (not approved), cancelled (voided before posting).
Unique identifier for the journal entry line item. Primary key for granular GL transaction tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Line Item Schema

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Link line to cost_center master to normalize cost center details.
Reference to the fixed asset master record if this line represents a fixed asset transaction (acquisition, depreciation, disposal). Null for non-FA entries.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Replace gl_account_code with authoritative FK to gl_account for detailed account attributes.
Reference to the parent journal entry header. Links this line to the overall accounting document.

Foreign key linking to realestate.lease. Business justification: Under IFRS 16/ASC 842, journal entry lines for ROU amortization, interest expense, and lease liability reduction must reference the specific lease. Lease accounting reconciliation and external audit r
Reference to the customer master record if this line represents an accounts receivable transaction. Null for non-AR entries.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Link line to profit_center master for consistent profit center data.
Transaction amount in the original document currency. Used for multi-currency transactions before conversion to local currency.
Transaction amount converted to the local reporting currency of the legal entity. Used for consolidated financial statements.
Free-text assignment field for additional reference information. Often used for invoice numbers, PO numbers, or internal tracking codes.
Baseline date for payment terms calculation. Typically the invoice date or goods receipt date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms Calculation

description: Business area for segment reporting. May represent franchise vs. company-owned, or QSR vs. casual dining divisions.
reference: IFRS 8 Operating Segments; SAP S/4HANA Business Area Configuration

description: Date on which this open item was cleared (paid or settled). Null if still open.
Document number of the clearing entry that settled this open item. Null if the line item is still open.
Four-character company code representing the legal entity. Required for multi-entity consolidation and statutory reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Configuration; GAAP Legal Entity Reporting

description: Indicates whether this line is a debit (D) or credit (C) entry. Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the document currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Date of the source document (invoice date, receipt date). May differ from posting date for backdated or future-dated entries.
reference: GAAP Source Document Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Document Date

description: Payment due date for AP/AR line items. Calculated based on posting date and payment terms. Null for non-payable/receivable entries.
Date on which the journal entry was created in the system. Audit trail for data entry timing.
Currency exchange rate applied to convert document currency to local currency. Null if currencies are the same.
Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, or 1-13 for year-end adjustments.
reference: GAAP Fiscal Period Standards; SAP S/4HANA Posting Period

description: Fiscal year to which this line item is posted. Derived from the posting date and fiscal calendar configuration.
reference: GAAP Fiscal Period Standards; SAP S/4HANA Fiscal Year Variant

description: Functional area classification for cost-of-sales accounting. Distinguishes COGS, labor, R&M, marketing, and administrative expenses.
reference: GAAP Functional Expense Classification; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Functional Area

description: Descriptive text explaining the purpose or nature of this journal entry line. Provides business context for the transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Line Item Text

description: Sequential line number within the journal entry. Determines the order of debit and credit entries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Line Item Numbering

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local reporting currency.
Payment terms code for AP/AR line items. Defines due date calculation and discount terms.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms Configuration

description: Date on which this line item was posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period assignment.
reference: GAAP Transaction Date Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Posting Date

description: Two-digit posting key that controls the account type (GL, vendor, customer, asset) and debit/credit indicator. SAP-specific control field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Posting Key Configuration

description: External reference document number (e.g., vendor invoice number, customer receipt number). Links GL entry to source transaction.
Indicates whether this line item is a reversal entry. True if this line reverses a prior posting.
Document number of the original entry that this line reverses. Null if not a reversal.
Single-character code for special GL transactions (e.g., down payments, guarantees, bills of exchange). Blank for standard postings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Special GL Transactions

description: Tax amount calculated for this line item in local currency. Null if the line is non-taxable.
Tax code applied to this line item. Determines sales tax, VAT, or GST treatment for revenue and expense transactions.
reference: IRS Publication 334; Local Sales Tax Regulations; SAP S/4HANA Tax Configuration

description: Trading partner code for intercompany transactions. Identifies the counterparty legal entity for consolidation and elimination entries.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Consolidation Standards; SAP S/4HANA Intercompany Accounting

description: SAP transaction code (T-code) used to create this entry (e.g., FB01, FB50, MIRO). Audit trail for process identification.
SAP user ID of the person who created this journal entry line. Audit trail for accountability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA User Master; SOX Audit Trail Requirements

description: Value date for cash management and bank reconciliation. Represents the effective date funds are available or debited.
Unique identifier for the legal entity record. Primary key.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Master

description: Foreign key reference to the parent legal entity in the corporate hierarchy. Null for the ultimate parent company.
Foreign key reference to the top-level parent entity in the corporate structure. Used for ultimate consolidation and group reporting.
